functions.name: App.CancelUserAttentionRequest
functions.description: Cancels a previous user attention request. A request is also canceled automatically by user activation of the app.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: The number for the user attention request.

functions.name: App.ClearTouchBar
functions.description: Clears a TouchBar assigned to the app. You assign the TouchBar with TouchBar.AssignToApp function.

functions.name: App.EffectiveUserID
functions.description: Queries effective user ID.

functions.name: App.Exit
functions.description: Quits the current process. Can be used on server to restart scripting engine. The engine then is restarted right away. On success, the next line in the script will not run.

parameters.name: ExitCode
parameters.description: The exit code to pass.

examples.label: Exit in script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("App.Exit")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.GetDockBadgeLabel
functions.description: Returns the tile's current badge label. Available in macOS v10.5 and later. iOS added in 7.2, but the badge is a number.

examples.label: Set and query dock badge:

$r
; Value:

App.SetDockBadgeLabel

functions.name: App.GetDockIcon
functions.description: Queries the dock icon. Only queries a dock item image you set before with App.SetDockIcon Returns nothing if no image was set.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: App.GetFocussedWindowTitle
functions.description: Queries the title of the focussed window, when run. This may be used in calculations triggered by context menu or hotkeys to know which window is front.

examples.label: Set hotkey to show dialog box with window title:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$hotkey
; Value:
MBS
("
HotKey.Register
"; "F5"; "") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
HotKey.SetEvaluate
";
HotKeys::HotKey
ID; "
MBS
( \"Msgbox\";
MBS
(\"App.GetFocussedWindowTitle\"))") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Hotkey.Register"; "F5"; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$hotkey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("HotKey.SetEvaluate"; HotKeys::HotKey ID; "MBS( \"Msgbox\"; MBS(\"App.GetFocussedWindowTitle\"))") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.GetProcessName
functions.description: Queries the name of the application. This value does not change by App.SetProcessName as it queries the true name. For FileMaker Pro, this is "FileMaker Pro", even if you have Advanced version.

functions.name: App.GetProcessPath
functions.description: Queries the path of the application. This value does not change by App.SetProcessName as it queries the file path.

examples.label: Get app folder path:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.RemoveLastPathComponent"; MBS( "App.GetProcessPath" ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.MemoryUsed
functions.description: Queries amount of memory used. This is to query the memory used in bytes for the current application. On Server this would query the scripting process. The value returned is not exactly the same as in Task Manager or Activity Monitor as this includes some shared memory for your process, which those don't count per app. See also App.CompactMemory for Windows to reduce memory.

examples.label: Query memory used
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$memory
; Value: Round(
MBS
("App.MemoryUsed") / 1024 / 1024; 0) & " MB" ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Round(MBS("App.MemoryUsed") / 1024 / 1024; 0) & " MB" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$memory</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.OpenFile
functions.description: Asks app to open a given file. This happens asynchronously, so we have no idea if it works or not. FileMaker may show error dialogs. This call must be last in a script as you can’t open a file while a script is running, the file should open when the script is done. You can use FM.RunScriptLater to run a script in a few seconds to follow up. There you can check with window functions and see if there is a new window in front. See also Files.Launch and Files.LaunchFile . Check also the Shell functions to run command line tools and get the output. This may work on the server, where the normal launch function may not work due to missing GUI. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to open.

examples.label: Open a database:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("App.OpenFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.fmp12") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.PlaySystemSound
functions.description: Plays a system sound with given name. Sound plays asynchronous. For macOS, you can go to /System/Library/Sounds folder and look for the sound names: Tink, Submarine, Sosumi, Purr, Pop, Ping, Morse, Hero, Glass, Funk, Frog, Bottle, Blow, Basso. For Windows look into registry at HKEY_CURRENT_USER\AppEvents\EventLabels section. Current list on Windows 11: ActivatingDocument, AppGPFault, BlockedPopup, CCSelect, ChangeTheme, Close, CriticalBatteryAlarm, DeviceConnect, DeviceDisconnect, DeviceFail, DisNumbersSound, EmptyRecycleBin, FaxBeep, FeedDiscovered, HubOffSound, HubOnSound, HubSleepSound, LowBatteryAlarm, MailBeep, Maximize, MenuCommand, MenuPopup, MessageNudge, Minimize, MisrecoSound, MoveMenuItem, Navigating, Notification.Default, Notification.IM, Notification.Looping.Alarm, Notification.Looping.Call, Notification.Mail, Notification.Proximity, Notification.Reminder, Notification.SMS, Open, PanelSound, PrintComplete, ProximityConnection, RestoreDown, RestoreUp, SecurityBand, ShowBand, SystemAsterisk, SystemExclamation, SystemExit, SystemHand, SystemNotification, SystemQuestion, WindowsLogoff, WindowsLogon, WindowsUAC, WindowsUnlock.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name to play.

examples.label: Play sound on macOS:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "App.PlaySystemSound"; "Submarine" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Play sound on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "App.PlaySystemSound"; "DeviceConnect" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: App.ProcessID
functions.description: Queries process ID. If it changes while your server runs, that means the scripting process crashed in-between.

functions.name: App.RequestUserAttention
functions.description: Starts a user attention request. Returns the identifier for the request. You can use this value to cancel the request later using the App.CancelUserAttentionRequest method. Activating the app cancels the user attention request. A spoken notification will occur if spoken notifications are enabled. Sending App.RequestUserAttention to an app that is already active has no effect. If the inactive app presents a modal panel, this method will be invoked with critical = 1 automatically. The modal panel is not brought to the front for an inactive app. This method is a way to have the dock icon jumping.

parameters.name: Critical
parameters.description: Pass 1 if critical or 0 if informative.

examples.label: Request attention:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "App.RequestUserAttention"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.Framerate
functions.description: Looks for first video track and reports the nominal frame rate. Actual frame rate may be variable.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

Set Variable [
$Asset
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.OpenFile
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" )]
Set Variable [
$Framerate
; Value:
MBS
( "AVAsset.Framerate";
$Asset
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$Asset
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["Framerate";
$Framerate
]

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.OpenFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" )</Calculation>

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: AVAsset.Image
functions.signature: MBS( "AVAsset.Image"; Asset; Time { ; ImageType; FileName; Width; Height } )
functions.description: Returns an image for the asset at or near a specified time. The time may not exactly be possible, so a nearby frame may be picked. You can call AVAsset.RequestImageTimePrecision before to request exact timings for images. Query AVAsset.ImageActualTime function to see which time the image was created from. If you need this functionality on Linux or Windows, you can use Shell functions and run ffmpeg command line to extract a frame for you.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.description: The position in the video in seconds.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The maximum width to use.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The maximum height to use.

functions.result: Returns container value with image or error.

examples.label: Queries image at 5 seconds:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Image"; $asset; 5; "jpeg"; "preview.jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Make a preview from video:
examples.text_code:
# try video via
AVAsset
functions
If [
$CouldBeVideo
]
Set Variable [
$asset
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.OpenContainer
";
$input
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "AVAsset.Image";
$asset
; 10; "jpeg"; "preview.jpg") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$image
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "AVAsset" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$asset
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$asset
) ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> try video via AVAsset functions</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $CouldBeVideo </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.OpenContainer"; $input ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$asset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Image"; $asset; 10; "jpeg"; "preview.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$image </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Preview "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"AVAsset" </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Made using "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $asset ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $asset ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.ImageActualTime
functions.description: Queries actual time for image. After a call to AVAsset.Image , this returns the time of the picture you got. Returns number of seconds.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

examples.label: Query image and time:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$a
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.OpenFile
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Trampolin.mov" ) ]
Set Field [
test::Image
;
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Image
";
$a
; 5; "JPEG"; "frame.jpg" ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Image requested at 5 seconds and got image from:" ;
MBS
( "AVAsset.ImageActualTime";
$a
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$a
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Image requested at 5 seconds and got image from:" ; MBS( "AVAsset.ImageActualTime"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$a ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $a ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.Loaded
functions.description: Queries load status of asset. Returns status like Loading, Loaded, Cancelled, Failed or Unknown. If status starts with "Failed: ", it is followed by a reason.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

examples.label: Load and check status:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Asset
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.OpenFile
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" )]
Set Variable [
$Loaded1
; Value:
MBS
( "AVAsset.Loaded";
$Asset
)]
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
Set Variable [
$Loaded2
; Value:
MBS
( "AVAsset.Loaded";
$Asset
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$Asset
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["Loaded";
$Loaded1
& ¶ &
$Loaded2
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.OpenFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Asset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Loaded"; $Asset )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Loaded1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Loaded"; $Asset )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Loaded2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $Asset )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Loaded"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$Loaded1 & ¶ &  $Loaded2</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.OpenContainer
functions.description: Opens a movie from a container. Should work for external and internal containers. Please allow a second to load video in background asynchronously. Available in OS X v10.7 and later. Please call AVAsset.Release later to free object from memory. You can use AVExport.ExportSession to export to a new file. With AVExport.SetTimeRange you can only export a clip and with AVExport.ExportSessionMerge you can merge multiple videos to one.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container with the movie file.

functions.result: Returns asset reference number or error.

examples.label: Open movie from container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.OpenContainer"; MyTable::MyContainer )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Close it later
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $asset )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.OpenFile
functions.description: Opens an asset from a file. Please allow a second to load video in background asynchronously. Available in OS X v10.7 and later. Please call AVAsset.Release later to free object from memory. You can use AVExport.ExportSession to export to a new file. With AVExport.SetTimeRange you can only export a clip and with AVExport.ExportSessionMerge you can merge multiple videos to one. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the movie file.

functions.result: Returns asset reference number or error.

examples.label: Opens a movie file:
examples.xml_code:

  </Step>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.RequestImageTimePrecision
functions.description: Requests to use high precision for timing of images. The actual time of the generated images will be within the range [requestedTime-toleranceBefore, requestedTime+toleranceAfter] and may differ from the requested time for efficiency. Pass 0 for both toleranceBefore and toleranceAfter to request frame-accurate image generation; this may incur additional decoding delay. Default is infinity.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

parameters.name: toleranceBefore
parameters.description: Time tolerance in seconds before requested time.

parameters.name: toleranceAfter
parameters.description: Time tolerance in seconds after requested time.

examples.label: Request exact timing:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>

parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

parameters.name: TrackIndex
parameters.description: The track index from 0 to AVAsset.TrackCount -1.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to get.

examples.label: Query media subtype:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.TrackInfo"; $Asset; 0; "MediaSubType" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  asset = MBS( "AVAsset.OpenFile"; "/Users/cs/Downloads/test.mp3" );
  formatDescription = MBS( "AVAsset.TrackInfo"; Asset; 0; "formatDescription" );
  r = MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; Asset )
]; formatDescription)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.URL
functions.description: Queries the URL used to open this asset. Empty if no URL is available.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

functions.result: Returns URL as text or error.

examples.label: Queries URL used to open movie:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.URL"; $asset)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVAsset.WaitForLoaded
functions.description: Waits for file to be loaded. Returns status like Loading, Loaded, Cancelled, Failed or Unknown. If status starts with "Failed: ", it is followed by a reason. While AVAsset.Loaded returns directly and may return "Loading", this function can be called after AVAsset.OpenFile , AVAsset.OpenURL or AVAsset.OpenContainer to have the script wait for assets to load.

parameters.name: Asset
parameters.description: The reference number of the asset. Please use AVAsset.OpenContainer , AVAsset.OpenFile or AVAsset.OpenURL function to get asset reference.

examples.label: Load asset:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Movies/video.mov" ]
Set Variable [
$asset
; Value:
MBS
("
AVAsset.OpenFile
";
$path
) ]
# now wait a few milliseconds to load
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
( "AVAsset.WaitForLoaded";
$asset
) ]
# check status
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Loaded
";
$asset
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Loaded" ;
$r1
& ¶ &
$r2
]
Set Variable [
$r3
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVAsset.Release
";
$asset
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Movies/video.mov" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("AVAsset.OpenFile"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$asset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now wait a few milliseconds to load</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.WaitForLoaded"; $asset ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check status</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Loaded"; $asset ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Loaded" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r1 & ¶ & $r2 </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVAsset.Release"; $asset ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r3</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Bonjour.Browse
functions.description: Starts browse for given type. Results from last browse are cleared first. You need Bonjour library installed on Windows to have this work. Use Bonjour.JSON functions to query results later. Please let a small pause for network to provide results, which get updated in background.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type to browse for.

examples.label: Browser for SMB shares:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Bonjour.Browse"; "_smb._tcp.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Browser for AFP shares:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Bonjour.Browse"; "_afpovertcp._tcp.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Browser for FileMaker server:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Bonjour.Browse"; "_fmpro-rcfmp12._tcp.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Bonjour.JSON
functions.description: Query current results as JSON. First level in JSON is an array with services found. For each service, we list interfaceIndex, type, domain, serviceName, IP and port. IP and port are brought down from found host and IP. Hosts array for each service describes the hosts for that service. That can be one or more hosts. Each host may have several IPs to connect to.

examples.label: Query results:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Bonjour.JSON" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Bonjour.Register
functions.signature: MBS( "Bonjour.Register"; Name; Type; Domain; Host; Port )
functions.description: Register a service. This service is discovered via Bonjour.Browse function.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: If non-empty, specifies the service name to be registered. Most applications will not specify a name, in which case the computer  name is used If a name is specified, it must be 1-63 bytes of UTF-8 text. If the name is longer than 63 bytes it will be automatically truncated to a legal length.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The service type followed by the protocol, separated by a dot (e.g. "_ftp._tcp"). The service type must be an underscore, followed by 1-14 characters, which may be letters, digits, or hyphens. The transport protocol must be "_tcp" or "_udp". New service types  should be registered at http://www.dns-sd.org/ServiceTypes.html.

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: If non-empty, specifies the domain on which to advertise the service. Most applications will not specify a domain, instead automatically registering in the default domain(s).

parameters.name: Host
parameters.description: If non-empty, specifies the SRV target host name. Most applications will not specify a host, instead automatically using the machine'sdefault host name(s).

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The port, in network byte order, on which the service accepts connections. Pass 0 for a "placeholder" service (i.e. a service that will not be discovered by browsing, but will cause a name conflict if another client tries to register that same name).  Most clients will not use placeholder services.

examples.label: Register test service:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Bonjour.Register"; "Test"; "_test._tcp"; ""; ""; 1234 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Bonjour.RegisteredName
functions.description: Queries registered name. Returns the full name of the service registered currently. This function checks for a license. Created 31st July 2018 , last changed 31st July 2018 Bonjour.Register - CF 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: ClearErrors
functions.description: Clears error counter. Call this on the beginning of a script where you like to track errors.

functions.name: Clipboard.AddText
functions.description: Adds text to the clipboard. This function allows on macOS to put multiple things on the clipboard, like both a text representation and a xml one. So if user pastes to a text editor, they get the text editor, but if they paste into your app, you can get back the xml.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new txt for the clipboard.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type. By default NSStringPboardType, but can be other Mac types.

examples.label: Add two text things and query back:
examples.text_code:
#  set
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Clipboard.SetText
"; "Hello World" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Clipboard.AddText"; "{\"text\": \"Hello World\", \"RowID\": 123 }"; "JSON" ) ]
#  query
Set Variable [
$$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
Clipboard.GetText
"; "JSON") ]
Set Variable [
$$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
Clipboard.GetText
"; "") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  set</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.SetText"; "Hello World" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.AddText"; "{\"text\": \"Hello World\", \"RowID\": 123 }"; "JSON" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  query</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.GetText"; "JSON") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.GetText"; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$text</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add both xml and text to clipbord:
examples.text_code:
# put script step on clipboard
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Clipboard.SetFileMakerData
"; "auto"; "<fmxmlsnippet type=\"FMObjectList\"><Step enable=\"True\" id=\"62\" name=\"Pause/Resume Script\"><PauseTime value=\"ForDuration\"></PauseTime><Calculation><![CDATA[1]"&"]></Calculation></Step></fmxmlsnippet>" ) ]
# and a text for Text editors
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Clipboard.AddText"; "Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]"; "" ) ]
# now you can paste in code editor
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> put script step on clipboard</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.SetFileMakerData"; "auto"; "<fmxmlsnippet type=\"FMObjectList\"><Step enable=\"True\" id=\"62\" name=\"Pause/Resume Script\"><PauseTime value=\"ForDuration\"></PauseTime><Calculation><![CDATA[1]"&"]></Calculation></Step></fmxmlsnippet>" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and a text for Text editors</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.AddText"; "Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now you can paste in code editor</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.Clear
functions.description: Clears the clipboard content. If you would use Clipboard.SetText with an empty text, you would still have text in the list of formats on the clipboard with no content. With Clipboard.Clear you get a truly empty clipboard.

functions.name: Clipboard.DetectFileMakerDataType
functions.description: Checks which type of data is in the XML. Returns the type as text: ValueList, Script, ScriptStep, Layout, CustomFunction, Table, Field or Layout12. Added new types Menu and MenuSet for MBS Plugin in version 12.2.

parameters.name: XML Text
parameters.description: The xml text to put on the clipboard.

functions.name: Clipboard.GetData
functions.description: Queries data from clipboard item. We look at the clipboard for a matching item with given type and return it in the format type you request. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client.

parameters.name: ClipboardType
parameters.description: The clipboard type to query. On Windows can also be a number.

parameters.name: ResultType
parameters.description: How to return the data. Can be "hex" for hex encoded, "base64" for base 64 encoded, "text" for trying to read data as text, "file" for a container value containing a file or "image" for reading data as image.

parameters.name: Param1
parameters.description: For ResultType "file" the filename of the container value. For ResultType "image" the image type. Can be PNG, JPEG, GIF or BMP.

parameters.name: Param2
parameters.description: For ResultType "image", the file name of the image in the container value.

examples.label: Queries icon from clipboard:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.GetData"; "com.apple.icns"; "image")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.GetFileMakerData
functions.description: Queries the xml text of a FileMaker layout/script object. The result is a XML text which you can store for later. The type can be ValueList, Script, ScriptStep, Layout, CustomFunction, Table, Field or Layout12. Pass auto as type in version 10.1 or newer to let the plugin look for all types and return the data found. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. Added type ValueList for FileMaker 16. For custom menus in FileMaker 17, please use Clipboard.GetText function. Warning: Some settings in script step for import, export or printer setup may not be preserved in copied content!

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: What element type to query. Can be "auto" for version 10.1 for automatic type selection.

functions.name: Clipboard.GetFiles
functions.description: Queries file paths on the clipboard. If you copy files in Finder or Explorer, you can use this plugin function to get a list of files. Returns native file paths, so you may need Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath function. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client.

examples.label: Query list of files:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.GetFiles" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.GetHTMLText
functions.description: Queries styled text from clipboard as HTML. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. For macOS, if a webarchive is on the clipboard, the plugin will pick data from that webarchive. Safari puts a Webarchive on the clipboard if you do a copy to the clipboard. If text encoding is UTF-8, the plugin will automatically use this encoding as default unless you overwrite it.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is Windows ANSI. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Clipboard.GetPDF
functions.description: Queries PDF in the clipboard. You can put result in variable or assign to a field. Returns empty value if clipboard does no contain PDF data.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the container value. Default is clipboard.pdf

functions.name: Clipboard.GetPicture
functions.description: Queries a picture from clipboard. You can store image in a container field for example.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: Clipboard.GetRTFText
functions.description: Queries styled text from clipboard as RTF. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is Windows ANSI. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Clipboard.GetStyledText
functions.description: Queries styled text from clipboard. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. Implemented for Windows with version 11.3 of MBS Plugin. If there is no styled text, we return empty text and you may use Clipboard.GetText then.

functions.name: Clipboard.GetText
functions.description: Queries clipboard content as text. Examples for type parameter: public.utf8-plain-text, CorePasteboardFlavorType 0x54455854, public.rtf,CorePasteboardFlavorType 0x75747874. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. You may want to use Text.ReplaceNewLine function with mode = 1 on the text to normalize line endings to what FileMaker expects. To query FileMaker’s XML for ValueList, Script, ScriptStep, Layout, CustomFunction, Table or Field, please check Clipboard.GetFileMakerData function.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type. By default NSStringPboardType, but can be other Mac types. Mac only.

functions.result: The text from the clipboard. Empty if no text is there.

examples.label: Get text from clipboard:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.GetText")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query addressbook data from Contacts app:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.GetText"; "ABPeopleDictPboardType")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get design from clipboard after copying it in FileMaker:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.GetText"; "dyn.agk8u")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.GetTypes
functions.description: Queries the list of data types on the clipboard. The plugin currently only get/set unicode text using Clipboard.GetText / Clipboard.SetText . If you need additional data types supported, please contact us. For server this will query the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client.

examples.label: Query list of types:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.GetTypes" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.SetFileMakerData
functions.description: Puts the xml text on the clipboard for a FileMaker layout/script object. Be sure to put the correct xml on the clipboard to avoid FileMaker being confused. The type can be ValueList, Script, ScriptStep, Layout, CustomFunction, Table, Field or Layout12. Pass auto as type in version 10.1 or newer to let the plugin look at the xml to decide on type. For server this will modify the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. Added type ValueList for FileMaker 16. For custom menus in FileMaker 17, please use Clipboard.SetText function.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: What element type to set. Can be "auto" for version 10.1 for automatic type selection.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to put on the clipboard.

examples.label: Put xml back on clipboard:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.SetFileMakerData"; "auto"; $xml ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.SetFiles
functions.description: Puts a list of file paths on the clipboard. For server this will modify the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. You can use Files.DeleteLater to delete temporary files later if needed. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Paths
parameters.description: The list of file paths.

examples.label: Put a file path to clipboard on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.SetFiles"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test.fmp12")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Put two file paths on clipboard on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

examples.label: Write a temp file and put it on the clipboard:
examples.text_code:
# find a temp file path
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" ); "export.jpg") ]
# write our container there
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.WriteFile
";
Test::GImage
;
$path
) ]
# and use it for clipboard

$result
; Value:

( "Clipboard.SetFiles";
$path
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find a temp file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ); "export.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write our container there</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; Test::GImage; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and use it for clipboard</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.SetFiles"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.SetHTMLText
functions.description: Puts HTML data on the clipboard. For server this will modify the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. On Windows you may want to use Text.ReplaceNewLine first to convert line endings. FileMaker on Windows uses Mac line endings internally.

parameters.name: HTMLText
parameters.description: The html data to put in clipboard.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is Windows ANSI. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Put some HTML on clipboard:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Clipboard.SetHTMLText"; "<b>Hello</b>"; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.SetStyledText
functions.description: Put styled text on the clipboard. This is direct way for styled text without converting in-between to RTF. For Windows or cross platform solutions you can combine Text.TextToRTF and Clipboard.SetRTFText . For server this will modify the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. Implemented for Windows with version 11.3 of MBS Plugin.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The styled text to put in clipboard.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Clipboard.SetText
functions.description: Sets the text on the clipboard. For server this will modify the clipboard of the user account running the server app on the server computer and not the clipboard of any client. On Windows you may want to use Text.ReplaceNewLine first to convert line endings. FileMaker on Windows uses Mac line endings internally. If called on Web Direct, the plugin puts text on the clipboard for the server, not for the client! So the functions are of no use on server.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new txt for the clipboard.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type. By default NSStringPboardType, but can be other Mac types.

examples.label: Sets text:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreImage.Detect
functions.description: Detects core image features. This function can do face recognition, find text or rectangle areas and read barcodes. Result is a JSON structure with all the results.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image to check. Pass container here with image.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of recognizer to use. Can be Face, Rectangle, Text or QRCode.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: List of options. If EyeBlink is included, facial expressions, such as blinking and closed eyes are extracted. If Smile is included, facial expressions, such as smile are extracted.

functions.name: Email.Verify
functions.description: Verifies if an email is valid. Check syntax of email and optionally verifies if domain is registered for email delivery. The network check may take a few seconds. While the function returns normally after a few milliseconds, the DNS server may not always be reachable and then the DNS query is sent several times. For DNS query to work, firewall must allow port 53 to be used to connect to DNS server. You can send or receive emails with our CURL functions. For sending the SendMail functions help to build an email and send it, while EmailParser functions help to parse an email. If you have a DNS server, which will redirect all mistyped domain names to a search website, this won’t really work well and detect too many valid domain names. Does not work for IDN domains, e.g. with umlauts. You need to transcode them to puny code yourself.

parameters.name: emailAddress
parameters.description: The email address to check.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The sum of the flags to use. Add 1 to check via DNS if domain is valid for email. Add 2 to return the name of the mail server domain.

examples.label: Checks an Email:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Email.Verify"; "Hello@macsw.de"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FontManager.ImageWithSystemSymbolName
functions.signature: MBS( "FontManager.ImageWithSystemSymbolName"; Name { ; Width; Height; ImageType; FileName } )
functions.description: Queries SF Symbol font for characters and returns them as image. This function needs macOS 11.0 or iOS 14. On iOS ignores size and returns font with native size. On macOS if either height or width is empty/zero, we calculate the other value with proportional scaling. See website for list of symbols: https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/sf-symbols/overview/

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the symbol

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width to use.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The image height to use.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

examples.label: Query picture with 512 pixel width:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("FontManager.ImageWithSystemSymbolName"; "icloud"; 512; ""; "png"; "test.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: FileName

functions.name: ImageCapture.Devices
functions.description: Queries list of devices. Returns a list of UUIDs for devices. Please pass the UUIDs to the ImageCapture.DeviceInfo function to query details. Before calling this function, make sure you called ImageCapture.Initialize once, best in your start script. Version 10.0 or later filters duplicates away.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: You can filter devices by passing criteria here. The filter string can contain camera, scanner, local, remote, bonjour, bluetooth or shared to only show those devices.

examples.label: List devices:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Devices
; Value:
MBS
("ImageCapture.Devices")]
#loop over entries
Set Variable [
$Count
; Value:ValueCount(
$Devices
)]
If [
$Count
> 0]
Set Variable [
$Index
; Value:1]
Loop
#Check Device
Set Variable [
$ID
; Value:GetValue(
$Devices
;
$index
)]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Devices::Name
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "name")]
Set Field [
Devices::ID
;
$ID
]
Set Field [
Devices::Remote
; Let ( [ r =
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "remote") ]; If(r = 1; "yes"; "no") )]
Set Field [
Devices::Shared
; Let ( [ r =
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "shared") ]; If(r = 1; "yes"; "no") )]
Set Field [
Devices::transportType
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "transportType")]
Set Field [
Devices::serialNumberString
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "serialNumberString")]
Set Field [
Devices::locationDescription
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "locationDescription")]
Set Field [
Devices::persistentIDString
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "persistentIDString")]
Set Field [
Devices::icon
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "icon")]
Set Field [
Devices::capabilities
;
MBS
("
ImageCapture.DeviceInfo
";
$ID
; "capabilities")]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
#next
Set Variable [
$Index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$Count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.Devices")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Devices</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>loop over entries</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($Devices)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$Count > 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check Device</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($Devices; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "name")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$ID</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="ID"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Let ( [ r = MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "remote") ]; If(r = 1; "yes"; "no") )</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="Remote"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Let ( [ r = MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "shared") ]; If(r = 1; "yes"; "no") )</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="Shared"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "transportType")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="transportType"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "serialNumberString")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="serialNumberString"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "locationDescription")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="locationDescription"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "persistentIDString")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="persistentIDString"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "icon")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="icon"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; $ID; "capabilities")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Devices" id="99999" name="capabilities"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index > $Count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query devices and names:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of devices
	list = MBS( "ImageCapture.Devices" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each device
	name = MBS( "ImageCapture.DeviceInfo"; id; "name" ); 
	names = names & id & " -> " & name & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.DevicesJSON
functions.description: Queries list of devices as JSON. The JSON includes names & IDs of the devices. Please pass the index or UUIDs to the ImageCapture.DeviceInfo function to query details. Keys include UUIDString, Name, persistentIDString, serialNumberString, locationDescription, index, remote and shared.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: You can filter devices by passing criteria here. The filter string can contain camera, scanner, local, remote, bonjour, bluetooth or shared to only show those devices.

examples.label: Check devices:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.Download
functions.description: Downloads a file from device. On success returns native file path of downloaded file. You can import file e.g. with Files.ReadFile . You can use ImageCapture.MediaFilenames to learn list of file names.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the media file to download.

parameters.name: Folder
parameters.description: Path of destination folder. If empty, we use temporary folder.

parameters.name: DeleteAfterDownload
parameters.description: Pass 1 to delete file on device after successful download.

functions.name: ImageCapture.ImagePaths
functions.description: Queries list of image files. After you scanned, you can use this function to query list of image file paths. File paths are returned as native file paths. Please use Container.ReadFile to read file and Files.Delete to delete them if needed.

examples.label: Query images and import them:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Paths
; Value:
MBS
("ImageCapture.ImagePaths")]
Set Variable [
$Count
; Value:ValueCount (
$Paths
)]
Set Variable [
$Index
; Value:1]
Loop
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value:GetValue(
$Paths
;
$index
)]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Images::Image
;
MBS
("
Container.ReadFile
";
$path
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.Delete
";
$Path
)]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$Index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.ImagePaths")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Paths</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $Paths )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($Paths; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $path)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Images" id="99999" name="Image"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.Delete"; $Path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index > $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes Image Capture engine. The plugin will now browse for devices and wait till at least till all local devices have been found. Network devices may be discovered a second later. So call this in your start script or first time user goes to scanning. Device count may change over time as devices are discovered or go away.

examples.label: Initialize:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ImageCapture.Initialize")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Initialize";
$r
]
Exit Script []
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.Initialize")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Initialize"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.MediaFilenames
functions.description: Queries file names list. All media filenames on the device as one big list.

functions.name: ImageCapture.MediaFiles
functions.description: Queries all image, movie and audio files on the camera. These files are returned as a single JSON array without regard to the folder hierarchy used to store these files on the camera. The plugin provides all details as a big JSON block for your analyzes.

functions.name: ImageCapture.OpenDevice
functions.description: Requests to open a session on the device. A client MUST open a session on a device in order to use the device. If needed closes current device to open new device. Added 20 seconds timeout for version 8.1. With ImageCapture.DeviceInfo you can query hasOpenSession later to verify you have a session open.

parameters.name: Device
parameters.description: The device ID. Can be zero based index in device list. Or UUID, Name or persistent ID string.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait till complete. Or pass 0 to return immediately. Default is 1 to wait.

examples.label: Open device:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ImageCapture.OpenDevice";
Images::Device
)]
If [
MBS
("iserror")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to open";
MBS
("
Text.RemovePrefix
";
$r
; "[
MBS
] ")]
Exit Script []
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.OpenDevice"; Images::Device)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("iserror")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Failed to open"; MBS("Text.RemovePrefix"; $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"[MBS] ")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestDeleteFiles
functions.description: Deletes files.

parameters.name: FileNames
parameters.description: The list of filenames.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait till complete. Or pass 0 to return immediately. Default is 1 to wait.

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestDisableTethering
functions.description: Disables tethering. Send this message to disable tethered capture on the camera device if the camera has the ICCameraDeviceCanTakePicture capability and if your process has already sent a requestEnableTethering with ImageCapture.RequestEnableTethering function to it.

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestEnableTethering
functions.description: Enable tethering. Send this message to enable tethered capture on the camera device if the camera has the ICCameraDeviceCanTakePicture capability.

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestOverviewScan
functions.description: Starts an overview scan on selectedFunctionalUnit. If a progress dialog from our plugin is visible, the plugin will keep it updated with progress. MBS Plugin 8.5 and newer observes progress and returns timeout error if scanner didn't do anything for 60 seconds.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait till complete. Or pass 0 to return immediately. Default is 1 to wait.

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestScan
functions.description: Starts a scan on selectedFunctionalUnit. If a progress dialog from our plugin is visible, the plugin will keep it updated with progress. Please use ImageCapture.SetParameter to configure scanner before requesting the scan, e.g. for duplex. MBS Plugin 8.5 and newer observes progress and returns timeout error if scanner didn't do anything for 60 seconds. Please call ImageCapture.OpenDevice to open device before requesting a scan.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait till complete. Or pass 0 to return immediately. Default is 1 to wait.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The timeout to wait for the scan to complete. Default is 60 seconds.
