<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set parameters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.GetParameter"; "physicalWidth")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$physicalWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.GetParameter"; "physicalHeight")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$physicalHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "scanArea"; 0; 0; $physicalWidth; $physicalHeight)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "documentType"; "USLetter")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "documentName"; "Scan")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "resolution"; 300)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "bitDepth"; 8)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "pixelDataType"; "RGB")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "documentUTI"; "jpeg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "downloadsDirectory"; "temp")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.Reset")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetTitle"; "Scanning" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetBottomText"; "" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetTopText"; "Scan from Flatbed scanner." )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetButtonCaption"; "Cancel" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; -1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.Show" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.requestScan")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.Hide" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("iserror")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Failed start scan"; MBS("Text.RemovePrefix"; $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"[MBS] ")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.ImagePaths")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Paths</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $Paths )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($Paths; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $path)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Images" id="99999" name="Image"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.Delete"; $Path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index > $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $path) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Images" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.Delete"; $Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestSelectFunctionalUnit
functions.description: Requests the scanner device to select a functional unit.

parameters.name: Unit
parameters.description: The functional unit to request. Can be Flatbed, PositiveTransparency, NegativeTransparency or DocumentFeeder.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait till complete. Or pass 0 to return immediately. Default is 1 to wait.

examples.label: Request flatbed unit:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ImageCapture.requestSelectFunctionalUnit"; "Flatbed")]
If [
MBS
("isError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to get flatbed?";
$r
]
Exit Script []
End If

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestSyncClock
functions.description: Synchronizes camera clock. Synchronize camera's clock with the computer's clock. You should send this request only if the camera has the ICCameraDeviceCanSyncClock capability.

functions.name: ImageCapture.RequestTakePicture
functions.description: Capture a new image using the camera. Works only if the camera capabilities include ICCameraDeviceCanTakePicture. You MUST call ImageCapture.RequestEnableTethering before requesting take picture. You may need to add a script pause after this to let the camera do the work. Confirmed to work in August 2020 to work on macOS Catalina with my Canon EOS 760D.

functions.name: ImageCapture.SetFeatureValue
functions.description: Sets a vendor scanner feature value.

parameters.name: FeatureName
parameters.description: The name of the feature.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: What value to set. Can be "value" for boolean values or "currentValue" for range or enumeration values.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value.

functions.name: ImageCapture.SetItemsAddedScript
functions.description: Sets script to call when new picture is added. The script is called and you get passed a JSON with an array of items.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set NewFileAdded script to be triggered when picture is taken:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ImageCapture.SetItemsAddedScript"; Get(FileName); "NewFileAdded" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.SetParameter"; "scanArea"; 0; 0; $physicalWidth; $physicalHeight) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Reset") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetTitle"; "Scanning" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetBottomText"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetTopText"; "Scan from Flatbed scanner." ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetButtonCaption"; "Cancel" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; -1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Show" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.requestScan") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Hide" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("iserror") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed start scan" ; MBS("Text.RemovePrefix"; $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"[MBS] ") </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read images</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImageCapture.ImagePaths") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Paths</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $Paths ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $Paths ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $Count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($Paths; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $path) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Images" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.Delete"; $Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImageCapture.Thumbnail
functions.description: Queries thumbnail for a file if available.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the media file to download.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: MessageComposer.AddAttachmentContainer
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a container field. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to attach to email.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The UTI, file extension or mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream".

examples.label: Adds an attachment:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$FieldName
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
Set Variable [
$Container
; Value: GetField (
$FieldName
) ]
If [ IsEmpty (
$container
) = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: GetAsText (
$Container
) ]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$path
) ]
Set Variable [
$extension
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.FileExtension
";
$name
) ]
Set Variable [
$mimeType
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.FileExtensionToMimeType
";
$extension
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MessageComposer.AddAttachmentContainer";
$container
;
$name
;
$mimeType
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FieldName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetField ( $FieldName) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty ( $container ) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText ( $Container ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.FileExtension"; $name ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$extension</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.FileExtensionToMimeType"; $extension ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mimeType</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.AddAttachmentContainer"; $container; $name; $mimeType) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.AddAttachmentFile
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a file path. Attachments over 100 MB are not supported. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to include as attachment.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The UTI, file extension or mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream".

functions.name: MessageComposer.AttachmentCount
functions.description: Queries number of attachments.

functions.name: MessageComposer.CanSendAttachments
functions.description: Checks whether we can send attachments. Returns 1 if the user has set up the device for including attachments in messages. This function can be called before having a composer initialized.

functions.name: MessageComposer.CanSendSubject
functions.description: Checks whether we can send subject. Returns 1 if the user has set up the device for including subjects in messages. This function can be called before having a composer initialized.

functions.name: MessageComposer.CanSendText
functions.description: Checks whether we can send text. Returns 1 if the user has set up the device for sending text only messages. If the return value is 1, the client can set the recipients and body of the message. If the return value is 0, the client may notify the user of the failure, or the client may open an SMS URL via open URL command. This function can be called before having a composer initialized.

examples.label: Check whether message can be send:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MessageComposer.CanSendText" ) ]
If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Can't send emails." ; "This iOS device is not configured to send text messages." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "failed" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.CanSendText" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Can't send emails." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"This iOS device is not configured to send text messages." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: "failed" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.Dismiss
functions.description: Dismisses the dialog. Something you can call in a script to cancel the message creation.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries the evaluation to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetFileName
functions.description: Queries the file name of the script to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetMessageBody
functions.description: Queries message body.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetRecipients
functions.description: Queries list of recipients.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name of the script to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: MessageComposer.GetSubject
functions.description: Queries subject text.

functions.name: MessageComposer.IsPresented
functions.description: Checks whether panel is displayed. Returns 1 if visible and 0 if not.

functions.name: MessageComposer.New
functions.description: Initializes the message composer panel. Resets last error and clears last message content. There is only one message composer at all times, so starting a new one will clear last one.

examples.label: Starts a new message:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MessageComposer.New" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MessageComposer.SetSubject
";
PlaceHolder::Subject
) ]

    </Repetition>

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.Result
functions.description: Queries result of email composer. If email composer is not yet finished, the result is empty. Status can be Cancelled, Sent, Failed.

examples.label: Check status:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "MessageComposer.Result" ) ]
Set Field [
PlaceHolder::Result
;
$result
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.Result" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to call when composer is done. In the script you can check with MessageComposer.Result what happened.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: MessageComposer.SetMessageBody
functions.description: Sets the body of the message to the specified content. This method will set the body of the email message.  This should be called prior to display. The user's signature, if specified, will be added after the body content.

parameters.name: Body
parameters.description: The text containing the body contents of the email message.

examples.label: Set the message text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.SetMessageBody"; "Hello World" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.SetRecipients
functions.description: Defines recipients. Sets the initial value of the To field for the message to the specified addresses.

parameters.name: Recipients
parameters.description: List of recipients.

examples.label: Send to two emails:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.SetRecipients"; $Email1 & ¶ & $Email2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when composer is done. In the script you can check with MessageComposer.Result what happened. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script to trigger.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to launch.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass to the script.

examples.label: Sets script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MessageComposer.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "Sent") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MessageComposer.SetSubject
functions.description: Sets the Subject header for the email message. This method will set the Subject header for the message.  This should be called prior to display. Newlines are removed from the parameter. After the view has been presented to the user, this method will no longer change the value.

parameters.name: Subject
parameters.description: The new subject.

functions.name: MessageComposer.disableUserAttachments
functions.description: Disable user attachments. Calling this method will disable the camera/attachment button in the view controller.  After the controller has been presented, this call will have no effect.  The camera / attachment button is visible by default.

functions.name: Schedule.Check
functions.description: Checks if something needs to be run. This is intended for server where you need to call this regularly (e.g. in scheduled script every minute) to process scheduled script calls or sql executes.

functions.name: Schedule.ClearEvaluateResult
functions.description: Clears result of last evaluate.

parameters.name: ScheduleRef
parameters.description: The reference for a scheduled script call or sql query as returned by Schedule.StartScript* functions or Schedule.ExecuteSQL* functions.

functions.name: Schedule.ClearSQLResult
functions.description: Clears result of last SQL query.

parameters.name: ScheduleRef
parameters.description: The reference for a scheduled script call or sql query as returned by Schedule.StartScript* functions or Schedule.ExecuteSQL* functions.

functions.name: Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; DelaySeconds; Expression { ; ScriptFileName; ScriptName; RepeatDelay } )
functions.description: Evaluates an expression after a delay. Optional, we can start a script with the result of the query. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: ScriptFileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run.

parameters.name: RepeatDelay
parameters.description: The number of seconds after which to repeat the script call.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$expression
; Value:"
MBS
(\"Log\"; \"Evaluate works\")"]
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 5;
$expression
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS(\"Log\"; \"Evaluate works\")"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$expression</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 5; $expression )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Open Scriptworkspace:
examples.text_code:
# won't work directly in script as we need to delay, let the script end and then the menu command can run:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay";
0.1
; "
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 51319 )") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> won't work directly in script as we need to delay, let the script end and then the menu command can run:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 0.1; "MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 51319 )") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Check in background for a file to appear and trigger script when it exists:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 5 ;

"Let ([ 

path = \"/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt\" ; 
databaseFile = \"Assets.fmp12\";
scriptName = \"Import\";
parameter = \"Your parameter\";

file = MBS(\"Files.FileExists\"; path);

r = If( file = 1;  MBS(\"FM.RunScript\"; databaseFile; scriptName; parameter); 0 )]; 0)";
""; ""; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Rename current script in Script Workspace:
examples.text_code:
Open Script Workspace
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Clipboard.SetText
"; "New Name") // Put new name on clipboard ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,1; "
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 52058 )" ) // Rename Script  ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,2; "
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 57637 )" ) // Paste ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,3; "
MBS
( \"
RemoteControl.PressKey
\"; 36; 13 )" ) // Press Return ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="88" name="Open Script Workspace"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Clipboard.SetText"; "New Name") // Put new name on clipboard </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,1; "MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 52058 )" ) // Rename Script  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,2; "MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57637 )" ) // Paste </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; ,3; "MBS( \"RemoteControl.PressKey\"; 36; 13 )" ) // Press Return </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.EvaluateAtTime
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAtTime"; Hours; Minutes; Seconds; Expression { ; ScriptFileName; ScriptName; RepeatHour; RepeatMinute; RepeatSecond } )
functions.description: Evaluates an expression at a given point in time. Optional, we can start a script with the result of the query. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. You define the timestamp with hours, minutes and seconds parameter at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy! Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules.

parameters.name: Hours
parameters.description: The hour of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: Minutes
parameters.description: The minute of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: Seconds
parameters.description: The seconds of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: ScriptFileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run.

parameters.name: RepeatHour
parameters.description: The number of hours to wait between script calls.

parameters.name: RepeatMinute
parameters.description: The number of minutes to wait between script calls.

parameters.name: RepeatSecond
parameters.description: The number of seconds to wait between script calls.

functions.name: Schedule.EvaluateAtTimeStamp
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAtTimeStamp"; TimeStamp; Expression { ; ScriptFileName; ScriptName; RepeatDelay } )
functions.description: Evaluates an expression at a given point in time. Optional, we can start a script with the result of the query. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. You define the timestamp at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy! Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy!

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: ScriptFileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run

parameters.name: RepeatDelay
parameters.description: The number of seconds after which to repeat the script call.

functions.name: Schedule.EvaluateOnIdle
functions.description: Evaluates an expression after a given idle period. This SQL query is performed in FileMaker whenever no user activity has been for the given amount of time. If script is called, the user is idle the given time period. Then once user starts working again, the count is reset and next time the user is idle again, the script can be called. So our idle schedules repeat automatically. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script. Moving the mouse or using keyboard will reset the counter.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: ScriptFileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run.

functions.name: Schedule.ExecuteSQLAfterDelay
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.ExecuteSQLAfterDelay"; DelaySeconds; FileName; SQL Statement { ; ScriptFileName; ScriptName; Column Delimiter; Record Delimiter; Params... } )
functions.description: Runs a SQL query in any open FileMaker file. Optional, we can start a script with the result of the query. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. Unless you call Schedule.Check to perform due schedules now, the schedules are performed at idle time, so all the SQL commands are available including INSERT, DELETE, UPDATE and even ALTER TABLE. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: ScriptFileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run.

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: Columns Separator. Default is tab character, which has unicode code point 9. You can pass delimiter as text (one character only) or as unicode code point (as number).

parameters.name: Record Delimiter
parameters.description: Record Separator. Default is return character, which has unicode code point 13. You can pass delimiter as text (one character only) or as unicode code point (as number).

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.description: Queries if scheduled item is removed from list of schedules automatically after execution. Value is 1 to auto remove from list of schedules or 0 if not.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
"; 5; "1+2")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.GetEvaluate";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

functions.name: Schedule.GetEvaluateResult
functions.description: Queries result of last evaluate. Please use Schedule.SetAutoRelease with parameter 0 to avoid the target schedule to be released before you can query result.

parameters.name: ScheduleRef
parameters.description: The reference for a scheduled script call or sql query as returned by Schedule.StartScript* functions or Schedule.ExecuteSQL* functions.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
# evaluate that in a second
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
"; 1; "1+2")]
# stop plugin from removing schedule after it was evaluated
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Schedule.SetFreeAfterRun";
$ref
; 0 )]
# check what is inside?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.GetEvaluate
";
$ref
)]
# make a pause
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): 2]
# now check result. Shows 3
Show Custom Dialog ["Result";
MBS
( "Schedule.GetEvaluateResult";
$ref
)]
# and free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> evaluate that in a second</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 1; "1+2")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> stop plugin from removing schedule after it was evaluated</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.SetFreeAfterRun"; $ref; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check what is inside?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.GetEvaluate"; $ref )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make a pause</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>2</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now check result. Shows 3</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Result"; MBS( "Schedule.GetEvaluateResult"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$ref )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.Release"; $ref )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.List
functions.description: Provides a list with all scheduled script starts or SQL commands. The list contains for each schedule the ID, type, file name, script name, script parameter, sql, repeat delay and idle delay.

parameters.name: OnlyIDs
parameters.description: Pass 1 to only get IDs. Pass 0 or no parameter to get IDs and details.

examples.label: List items

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$List</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.Release
functions.description: Releases schedule reference. This will free all memory used and stops the schedule.

parameters.name: ScheduleRef
parameters.description: The reference for a scheduled script call or sql query as returned by Schedule.StartScript* functions or Schedule.ExecuteSQL* functions.

examples.label: Release now:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.Release"; $ScheduleRef )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Start a script once in 10 seconds and release:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable[
$ref
;
MBS
( "
Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay
"; 10; Get(FileName); "testScript"; "Hello World" ) ]
Set Variable[
$r
;
MBS
("Schedule.Release";
$ref
) ]
# script will never be called as schedule was destroyed!
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay"; 10; Get(FileName); "testScript"; "Hello World" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Schedule.Release"; $ref) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> script will never be called as schedule was destroyed!</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.SetScriptName
functions.description: Sets the script name for scheduled script start. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: ScheduleRef
parameters.description: The reference for a scheduled script call or sql query as returned by Schedule.StartScript* functions or Schedule.ExecuteSQL* functions.

functions.name: Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay"; DelaySeconds; FileName; ScriptName { ; ScriptParameter; RepeatDelay } )
functions.description: Runs a script in any open FileMaker file. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: Optional, he Script Parameter to pass to the script

parameters.name: RepeatDelay
parameters.description: The number of seconds after which to repeat the script call.

examples.label: Start a script once in 10 seconds:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay"; 10; Get(FileName); "testScript"; "Hello World" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.StartScriptAtTime
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAtTime"; Hours; Minutes; Seconds; FileName; ScriptName { ; ScriptParameter; RepeatHour; RepeatMinute; RepeatSecond } )
functions.description: Runs a script in any open FileMaker file. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. You define the timestamp with hours, minutes and seconds parameter at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy! Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Hours
parameters.description: The hour of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: Minutes
parameters.description: The minute of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: Seconds
parameters.description: The seconds of the day at which to call the script.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: Optional, he Script Parameter to pass to the script

parameters.name: RepeatHour
parameters.description: The number of hours to wait between script calls.

parameters.name: RepeatMinute
parameters.description: The number of minutes to wait between script calls.

parameters.name: RepeatSecond
parameters.description: The number of seconds to wait between script calls.

examples.label: Run at 10:30 and repeat every 2 hour:

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAtTime"; 10; 30; 0; Get(FileName); "testScript"; ""; 2; 0; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>

functions.name: Schedule.StartScriptAtTimeStamp
functions.signature: MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAtTimeStamp"; TimeStamp; FileName; ScriptName { ; ScriptParameter; RepeatDelay } )
functions.description: Runs a script in any open FileMaker file. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list. Non repeating scheduled items are free automatically for you. You define the timestamp at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy! Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp at which to call the script. Plugin checks regularly if time stamp is reaches and calls as soon as current time is greater or equal the given timestamp. So calls can be delayed if FileMaker is busy!

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: Optional, he Script Parameter to pass to the script

parameters.name: RepeatDelay
parameters.description: The number of seconds after which to repeat the script call.

examples.label: Run script in 10 seconds and than repeat every 10 seconds:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.StartScriptAtTimeStamp"; Get(CurrentTimeStamp)+10; Get(FileName); "testScript"; "time passed"; 10 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Schedule.StartScriptOnIdle
functions.description: Runs a script after idle period. This script is called in FileMaker whenever no user activity has been for the given amount of time. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. If script is called, the user is idle the given time period. Then once user starts working again, the count is reset and next time the user is idle again, the script can be called. So our idle schedules repeat automatically. Please use Schedule.Release later to remove schedule from list when you don't need it anymore. Our plugin does not provide persistence. You need to schedule things in your scripts if you need them. When FileMaker quits, the plugin forgets the list of schedules. Once you have the script running, you can use Process.IsFrontMost function to check if FileMaker is in the foreground or not. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script. Moving the mouse or using keyboard will reset the counter.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: The Script Parameter to pass to the script

examples.label: Run a script 10 seconds after idle is detected:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$IdleSchedule
; Value:
MBS
("Schedule.StartScriptOnIdle"; 10; Get(FileName); "testScript"; "don't work?") ]
examples.xml_code:

  </Step>

functions.name: Shell.AddArgument
functions.description: Adds an argument. This argument is added to the list of arguments passed first on each call to Shell.Execute . With version 8.3, you can add several arguments here. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Argument parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Argument...
parameters.description: The new argument.

examples.label: Adds an argument:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.AddArgument"; $shell; "-v" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Send email via Outlook.exe with options via command line:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$app
; Value: "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Office\Office15\OUTLOOK.EXE" ]
Set Variable [
$attachment
; Value: "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\test.jpg" ]
Set Variable [
$to
; Value: "to@test.com" ]
Set Variable [
$subject
; Value: "Test Email" ]
Set Variable [
$cc
; Value: "copy@test.com" ]
Set Variable [
$body
; Value: "Hello World" ]
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
; "/c" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
; "ipm.note" ) ]
If [ not IsEmpty (
$attachment
) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
; "/a" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
;
$attachment
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
; "/m" ) ]
Set Variable [
$a1
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.EncodeURLComponent
";
$to
; "UTF-8" ) ]
Set Variable [
$a2
; Value: "?subject="  &
MBS
( "
Text.EncodeURLComponent
";
$subject
; "UTF-8" ) ]
Set Variable [
$a3
; Value: If ( Length (
$cc
) > 0 ; "&cc=" &
MBS
( "
Text.EncodeURLComponent
";
$cc
; "UTF-8" ); "" ) ]
Set Variable [
$a4
; Value: If ( Length (
$body
) > 0 ; "&body=" &
MBS
( "
Text.EncodeURLComponent
";
$body
; "UTF-8" ) ; "") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
;
$a1
&
$a2
&
$a3
&
$a4
) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Execute
";
$shell
;
$app
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>"Hello World" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$body</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$shell</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.AddArgument"; $shell; "/c" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.AddArgument"; $shell; "ipm.note" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"?subject="  & MBS( "Text.EncodeURLComponent"; $subject; "UTF-8" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If ( Length ( $cc ) > 0 ; "&cc=" & MBS( "Text.EncodeURLComponent"; $cc; "UTF-8" ); "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If ( Length ( $body) > 0 ; "&body=" & MBS( "Text.EncodeURLComponent"; $body ; "UTF-8" ) ; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a4</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.AddArgument"; $shell;  $a1 & $a2 & $a3 & $a4) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; $app) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add parameters various ways:

$shell
; Value:

# set some parameters to go always:

$r
;
MBS
( "
Shell.SetArguments
";
$shell
; "-a"; "test" )

If [
$Verbose
]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "Shell.AddArgument";
$shell
; "-v" )
End If
# now run command here and pass some more parameters

$s
; Value:

Shell.Execute

;
$executable
;
$InputFile
;
$OutputFile
) ]

examples.label: Run a command line tool with input:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Execute
";
$shell
; "/Applications/Postgres.app/Contents/Versions/15/bin/gdaltransform"; "-s_srs"; "EPSG:25832"; "-t_srs"; "EPSG:4326"; "-output_xy") ]
# send input data and close input
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.WriteInputText
";
$shell
; "666862 6716190"; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.CloseInput";
$shell
) ]
# wait for app to finish
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 5) ]
# read results
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Field [
Shell::Error
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$error
; 1) ]
Set Field [
Shell::Output
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$result
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]

functions.name: Shell.CloseStreams
functions.description: Closes all streams. This closes input, error and output streams.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.name: Shell.Execute
functions.description: Executes a child process. Parameters set by SetArgument* functions are passed first followed by any parameters you pass here. On macOS, we use NSTask. https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nstask On Linux we use execve function. https://www.unix.com/man-page/linux/3/execve/ On Windows we use CreateProcess function and we pass in a command line with the Executable parameter followed by all the arguments. If needed, we put them in quotes. https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms682425(v=vs.85).aspx If username is given for Windows, we use CreateProcessWithLogon function instead to provide login as a local user. https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms682431(v=vs.85).aspx If you just need to launch a file, please use Files.LaunchFile or Files.Launch . You can run "/usr/bin/whoami" on Mac/Linux or "whoami.exe" on Windows to print the user who executes the shell tool. For PowerShell scripts on Windows, you may need to check with the execution policy for the user account FileMaker is using or use an admin account. e.g. on FileMaker Server, may need to be installed as Admin user (not fmserver user). This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Arguments parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Executable
parameters.description: The path to the executable. Should be full path to executable. On Windows, the system also searches path if you just specify a file name.

parameters.name: Arguments...
parameters.description: Pass additional arguments to send.

examples.label: Run ffmpeg in shell to convert video to mp3 file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Field [
Shell::Output
; "" ]
Set Field [
Shell::Error
; "" ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
# Where the app is:
Set Variable [
$executable
; Value: "/Applications/ffmpegX.app/Contents/Resources/ffmpeg" ]
# option: overwrite file
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.AddArgument
";
$shell
; "-y" ) ]
# option: input file
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.AddArgument
";
$shell
; "-i" ) ]
# Path to input file
Set Variable [
$InputFile
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/movie.m4v" ]
# and output file
Set Variable [
$OutputFile
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/sound.mp3" ]
# Run it!
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.Execute";
$shell
;
$executable
;
$InputFile
;
$OutputFile
) ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value: "" ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run" ;
$s
]
Else
# Loop while app runs and collect messages
Loop
# Wait a second or till it quits
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 1) ]
# And read output
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
$error
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
$result
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Field [
Shell::Error
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$error
; 1) ]
Set Field [
Shell::Output
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$result
; 1) ]
# exit when done
Exit Loop If [
MBS
( "
Shell.IsRunning
";
$shell
) ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
# We are done
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]

  </Step>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> option: input file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.AddArgument"; $shell; "-i" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Path to input file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/movie.m4v" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputFile</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and output file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/sound.mp3" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OutputFile</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Run it!</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; $executable; $InputFile; $OutputFile) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$s </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Loop while app runs and collect messages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Wait a second or till it quits</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> And read output</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$error & MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$result & MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $error; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Error "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $result; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> exit when done</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS( "Shell.IsRunning"; $shell) ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> We are done</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
