examples.label: Start VPN on Mac:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.Execute";
$shell
; "/usr/sbin/scutil"; "--nc"; "start"; "VPN Name") ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value: "" ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run" ;
$s
]
Else
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 2) ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
If [ Length (
$error
) > 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "VPN Start" ;
$error
& ¶ &
$result
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$shell</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; "/usr/sbin/scutil"; "--nc"; "start"; "VPN Name") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$s </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $error ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "VPN Start" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$error & ¶ & $result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Zip files on FileMaker Cloud using Wait function waiting for 2 minutes maximum:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.Execute";
$shell
; "/usr/bin/zip"; "-r"; "/tmp/Logs.zip"; "/FileMakerData/Logs") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run" ;
$s
]
Else
Set Variable [
$running
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 120) ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$output
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
MBS
Test::Result
;
$output
]
Set Field [
MBS
Test::Error
;
$error
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]

examples.label: Zip files on FileMaker Cloud using IsRunning in a loop waiting for 2 minutes maximum:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.Execute";
$shell
; "/usr/bin/zip"; "-r"; "/tmp/Logs.zip"; "/FileMakerData/Logs") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to run" ;
$s
]
Else
Set Variable [
$error
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$running
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.IsRunning
";
$shell
) ]
Set Variable [
$output
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
$count
+ 1 ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
$error
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$output
; Value:
$output
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
( "
Shell.IsRunning
";
$shell
) ≠ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$count
> 1200 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
$error
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$output
; Value:
$output
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
MBS
Test::Result
;
$output
]
Set Field [
MBS
Test::Error
;
$error
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$shell</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; "/usr/bin/zip"; "-r"; "/tmp/Logs.zip"; "/FileMakerData/Logs") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$s </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.IsRunning"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$running</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$output</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$count + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$error & MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$output & MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$output</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS( "Shell.IsRunning"; $shell) ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $count > 1200 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$error & MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$output & MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$output</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$output </Calculation>
    <Field table="MBS Test" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$error </Calculation>
    <Field table="MBS Test" id="99999" name="Error "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run command in one Let statement:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
// new Shell
shell = MBS( "Shell.New" );
// run command with parameters
r = MBS( "Shell.Execute"; shell; "/bin/ls"; "/");
// wait for a result up to 5 seconds
r = MBS( "Shell.Wait"; shell; 5);
// read output
error = MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; shell; "UTF-8");
output = MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; shell; "UTF-8"); 
// free shell
r = MBS("Shell.Release"; shell)
]
; output & error )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query distribution description for Linux server:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
// new Shell
shell =
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" );
// run lsb release to print description for distribution
r =
MBS
( "Shell.Execute"; shell; "/usr/bin/lsb_release"; "-d");
// wait for a result up to 5 seconds
r =
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
"; shell; 5);
// read result
output =
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
"; shell; "UTF-8");
// free shell
r =
MBS
("
Shell.Release
"; shell)
]
; output )
Example result:
on CentOS 7.9 we get:
"Description:   CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)"
on Ubuntu 18.04 we get:
"Description:   Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS"

// read result

// free shell
r = MBS("Shell.Release"; shell)
]

Else
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 10) ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run shell in file Anlagen (fmsubuntu)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$shell</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; "/bin/bash"; "/opt/FileMaker/FileMaker Server/Data/Documents/test.sh") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to run" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$s </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 10) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Shell.New
functions.description: Creates a new shell object. Please call Shell.Release later. Technically the shell could run on iOS, but it does not due to sandboxing.

functions.name: Shell.PeekErrorText
functions.description: Peeks into error output buffer. Returns data in buffer without removing it. You need to call Shell.ReadErrorText later to actually read the data and remove it from the buffer. The plugin internally queries Shell.AvailableBytesError to see how much to read and return.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Shell.PeekOutputText
functions.description: Peeks into output buffer. Returns data in buffer without removing it. You need to call Shell.ReadOutputText later to actually read the data and remove it from the buffer. The plugin internally queries Shell.AvailableBytesOutput to see how much to read and return.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Shell.ProcessIdentifier
functions.description: Queries process identifier of child process. This function checks for a license. Created 11st November 2017 , last changed 11st November 2017 Shell.PeekOutputText - Shell.ReadErrorText 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.name: Shell.ReadErrorText
functions.description: Reads error. When child process writes something to error stream, you can read it here. The plugin internally queries Shell.AvailableBytesError to see how much to read and return.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Shell.ReadOutputText
functions.description: Reads output. When child process writes something to output stream, you can read it here. See also Shell.ReadErrorText for reading error output. The plugin internally queries Shell.AvailableBytesOutput to see how much to read and return. Some processes send data in chunks, so you have to read one chunk before you get the next one. So you may do a loop with calling Shell.Wait with e.g. 0.1 seconds. Check with Shell.IsRunning whether app is still running, so you can exit the loop if needed. And each time the loop runs, check if there is new output and read it.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Loop and collect messages:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$error
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value: "" ]
# Loop while app runs and collect messages
Loop
# Wait a second or till it quits
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 1) ]
# And read output
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
$error
&
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
$result
&
MBS
( "Shell.ReadOutputText";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Field [
Shell::Error
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$error
; 1) ]
Set Field [
Shell::Output
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$result
; 1) ]
# exit when done
Exit Loop If [
MBS
( "
Shell.IsRunning
";
$shell
) ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Loop while app runs and collect messages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Wait a second or till it quits</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> And read output</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$error & MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$result & MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $error; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Error "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $result; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> exit when done</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS( "Shell.IsRunning"; $shell) ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Shell.SetBufferSize

Windows may have an upper limit of 64K. Setting is ignore on MacOS as size is dynamic there. Please set before you execute something.

functions.name: Shell.SetCompletedScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when shell is done. The script parameter passed with the script call is the reference number of the shell object. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: Shell.SetCurrentDirectory
functions.description: Sets the current directory to use. Keep empty for default, which is current directory of FileMaker. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the new directory.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.result: Returns the reason the task was terminated.

functions.name: Shell.Wait
functions.description: Waits for child process. This blocks FileMaker for doing anything till the app is done or timeout happens. Please use 8.2 or newer on FileMaker Cloud as Shell.Wait/ Shell.IsRunning was broken there before.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The timeout in seconds on how long to wait.

examples.label: Wait 5 seconds or till the tool quit:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 5) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Shell.WriteInputText
functions.description: Writes a text to input stream. The child process and read this from the input stream. Please make sure you use right line endings and encoding. You must call Shell.Execute first to start the child process, before you send data.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Sends text to shell:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.WriteInputText"; $shell; "Hello World"; "UTF-8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write input with CR and LF on the end:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length(
Shell::Input
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.WriteInputText";
$shell
;
Shell::Input
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.WriteInputText";
$shell
; Char(13); "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.WriteInputText";
$shell
; Char(10); "UTF-8") ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length(Shell::Input) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $s ; Value: MBS( "Shell.WriteInputText"; $shell; Shell::Input; "UTF-8") ] 
	Set Variable [ $s ; Value: MBS( "Shell.WriteInputText"; $shell; Char(13); "UTF-8") ] 
	Set Variable [ $s ; Value: MBS( "Shell.WriteInputText"; $shell; Char(10); "UTF-8") ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run a command line tool with input:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$shell
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Execute
";
$shell
; "/Applications/Postgres.app/Contents/Versions/15/bin/gdaltransform"; "-s_srs"; "EPSG:25832"; "-t_srs"; "EPSG:4326"; "-output_xy") ]
# send input data and close input
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "Shell.WriteInputText";
$shell
; "666862 6716190"; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.CloseInput
";
$shell
) ]
# wait for app to finish
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.Wait
";
$shell
; 5) ]
# read results
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadErrorText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
Shell.ReadOutputText
";
$shell
; "UTF-8") ]
Set Field [
Shell::Error
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$error
; 1) ]
Set Field [
Shell::Output
;
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$result
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Shell.Release
";
$shell
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$shell</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Execute"; $shell; "/Applications/Postgres.app/Contents/Versions/15/bin/gdaltransform"; "-s_srs"; "EPSG:25832"; "-t_srs"; "EPSG:4326"; "-output_xy") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> send input data and close input</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.WriteInputText"; $shell; "666862 6716190"; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.CloseInput"; $shell ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> wait for app to finish</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.Wait"; $shell; 5) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read results</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadErrorText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Shell.ReadOutputText"; $shell; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $error; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Error "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $result; 1) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Shell" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Shell.Release"; $shell) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Speech.AvailableVoices
functions.description: Query the list of available voices. This returns the identifiers for voices.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Pass 1 to get list of identifiers. By default or pass 0 to get list of names.

examples.label: Query voices:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.AvailableVoices" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Speech.IsPaused
functions.description: Checks whether we are paused. Returns 1 if paused or 0 if not.

functions.name: Speech.IsSpeaking
functions.description: Queries whether text is speaking. Returns 1 if voice is speaking or 0 if not.

functions.name: Speech.Pause
functions.description: Pauses speech.

examples.label: Test pausing:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Speech.Speak
"; "Hello World, just a test") ]
Pause Script [ Duration (Seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Speech.Pause") ]
Pause Script [ Duration (Seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Speech.IsPaused
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Speech.Resume
") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Hello World, just a test") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Pause") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.IsPaused") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Resume") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Speech.Resume
functions.description: Resumes speech.

functions.name: Speech.Speak
functions.signature: MBS( "Speech.Speak"; Text { ; Voice; Wait; Volume; Rate } )
functions.description: Speaks text. This is a function doing similar work as the speak script step in FileMaker. If called with empty text, you can pass voice to test if voice exists. You get back OK if voice exists or an error if not. If called without text, you stop existing speech. Due to a bug in FM 13/14/15, the speak script step may freeze. This function is a suitable workaround until FileMaker fixes the issue. You can use Speech.IsSpeaking to check if asynchronously speaking is in use.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to speak

parameters.name: Voice
parameters.description: The voice to use. Can be empty to use default voice. On macOS and iOS, this is identifier, not name.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Whether to wait for completion. Pass 1 to wait or 0 to speak while your script continues. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.description: The volume to use. Default is full volume. Range from 0.0 (silent) to 1.0 (full).

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.description: The speak rate. Default 1.0 for normal speed. Range from 0.0 to 2.0. 2.0 would be double speed while 0.5 is half speed. If the voice doesn’t support rate, you hear no difference!

examples.label: Say Hello
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Hello World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Count to 100:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:0]
Loop
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+ 1]
Set Field [
Speak Test::Counter
;
$i
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Speech.Speak";
$i
; ""; 1)]
Commit Records/Requests []
Exit Loop If [
$i
> 100]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$i</Calculation>
    <Field table="Speak Test" id="99999" name="Counter"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; $i; ""; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$i > 100</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Speak with Windows voice:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Hello World"; "Microsoft Hedda Desktop - German"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Hallo Leute"; ""; 0; 0,5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

examples.label: Using embedded text commands on Mac:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Hello [[char LTRL]] cat [[char NORM]] cat."; ""; 1)
// literal speaking

MBS( "Speech.Speak"; "Do [[emph +]] not [[emph -]] over tighten the screw.")
// use emphasis:

// see
// https://developer.apple.com/library/content/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/SpeechSynthesisProgrammingGuide/FineTuning/FineTuning.html</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Speech.Stop
functions.description: Stops speech as soon as possible. May speak to end of word.

functions.name: WMFP.Create
functions.description: Creates a new player. The player can be audio only without showing in the layout or reference a layout object to play with video. The file is loaded asynchronously, so please wait a few milliseconds or check state later. Use WMFP.Release later to destroy the video player. Pass no window reference and no control name to create an audio player for background playback. See also AVPlayer.OpenContainer and AVPlayer.OpenFile for macOS and iOS.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL for the video. See Path.FilePathToFileURL function to convert file path.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

examples.label: Load video into new player:
examples.text_code:
# Create new player and load video
Set Variable [
$URL
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.FilePathToFileURL
";
Windows Player::Path
) ]
# Player is a rectangle on the layout
Set Variable [
$$player
; Value:
MBS
("WMFP.Create";
$URL
; 0; "Player") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load video" ;
$$player
]
Set Variable [
$$player
; Value: "" ]
Else
# Little pause to let loading happen in background
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WMFP.SetPlaybackEndedScriptTrigger
";
$$player
; Get(FileName); "Playback Done") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WMFP.Play
";
$$player
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Create new player and load video</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.FilePathToFileURL"; Windows Player::Path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$URL</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Player is a rectangle on the layout</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.Create"; $URL; 0; "Player") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$player</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to load video" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$$player </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$player</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Little pause to let loading happen in background</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.SetPlaybackEndedScriptTrigger"; $$player; Get(FileName); "Playback Done") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.Play"; $$player) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WMFP.GetVideoFrameRate
functions.description: Frame rate of a video media type, in frames per second. Requires a media item being loaded into the player. The frame rate is expressed as a ratio. The upper 32 bits of the attribute value contain the numerator and the lower 32 bits contain the denominator. For example, if the frame rate is 30 frames per second (fps), the ratio is 30/1. If the frame rate is 29.97 fps, the ratio is 30,000/1001.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

examples.label: Query it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$num
; Value:
MBS
("WMFP.GetVideoFrameRate";
$$player
) ]
Set Variable [
$t
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
";
$num
; 32) ]
Set Variable [
$b
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
$num
; 4294967295)]
Set Variable [
$value
; Value:
$t
/
$b
]
Example result:
29.97
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.GetVideoFrameRate"; $$player) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$num</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; $num; 32) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$t</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAnd"; $num; 4294967295)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$b</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$t / $b </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$$Player

; Value:

("WMFP.Release";

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 65535. Can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

  </Step>

functions.name: WMFP.SetPlaybackEndedScriptTrigger
functions.description: Sets the playback ended script trigger. The given script is triggered when player reaches the end of the video. Pass empty file and script name to clear.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

functions.name: WMFP.SetPosition
functions.description: Sets the position. Pass new position in seconds.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new position.

examples.label: Jump 10 seconds forward:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WMFP.SetPosition";
$$player
; 10 +
MBS
("
WMFP.GetPosition
";
$$player
)) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.SetPosition"; $$player; 10 + MBS("WMFP.GetPosition"; $$player)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Jump back to start:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WMFP.SetPosition"; $$player; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WMFP.SetRate
functions.description: Sets the current playback rate. The playback rate is expressed as a ratio of the current rate to the normal rate. For example, 1.0 indicates normal playback, 0.5 indicates half speed, and 2.0 indicates twice speed. Positive values indicate forward playback, and negative values indicate reverse playback.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new rate.

examples.label: Set to double speed:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WMFP.SetRate"; $$Player; 2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WMFP.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag string for this player. Use the tag to store whatever value you want to associate with the text view. You can later query it using WMFP.GetTag .

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value.

examples.label: Set tag:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WMFP.SetVisible
functions.description: Hides or shows the player. By default we create it visible, but you can hide it if it's on a register and the user clicked to other tab.

parameters.name: Player

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: The new visible state. Pass 0 to hide or 1 to show.

examples.label: Hide player, e.g. when switching layouts or registers:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Show player:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WMFP.SetVolume
functions.description: Sets the audio volume. Volume is expressed as an attenuation level, where 0.0 indicates silence and 1.0 indicates full volume (no attenuation). If you call this method before playback starts, the setting is applied after playback starts.

parameters.name: Player
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows Media Foundation Player object.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.description: The new volume value.

examples.label: Set to volume to 50%:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ApplyStylesheet
functions.description: Apply the stylesheet to the document (XSLT transform). Performs a XSLT transformation on the input document. The output may be a non-wellformed output XML wise! Returns various errors if XML is not well formed or style sheet is invalid. Based on LibXSLT, so it provides an implementation of XSLT 1.0, plus most of the EXSLT set of processor-portable extensions functions and some of Saxon's evaluate and expressions extensions. Added exsl functions for MBS Plugin in version 15.0. If you need XSLT 2.0/3.0, please check the Saxon.XSLT function.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: StyleSheet
parameters.description: The XML of the style sheet. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.description: The list of parameters to pass to the engine. Pass a list with key=value format.

examples.label: Apply a stylesheet:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$InputXML
; Value:
XSLT::InputXML
]
Set Variable [
$StyleSheet
; Value:
XSLT::StyleSheet
]
Set Variable [
$Output
; Value:
MBS
("XML.ApplyStylesheet";
$InputXML
;
$StyleSheet
) ]
Set Field [
XSLT::OutputXML
;
$Output
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>XSLT::InputXML </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputXML</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>XSLT::StyleSheet </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$StyleSheet</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.ApplyStylesheet"; $InputXML; $StyleSheet) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Output</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$Output </Calculation>
    <Field table="XSLT" id="99999" name="OutputXML"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ApplyStylesheet2
functions.description: Apply the stylesheet to the document (XSLT transform). Same as XML.ApplyStylesheet , but with different parameter passing. Optionally you can pass keys and values as parameters to this function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Value parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: StyleSheet
parameters.description: The XML of the style sheet. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The parameter key name.

parameters.name: Value...
parameters.description: The parameter value. This may be quoted with single quotes.

examples.label: Run XLST with parameters:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.ApplyStylesheet2" ; $InputXML ; $XSLTransform ; "id"; "'123'"; "name"; "'Bob'")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run XLST without parameters:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.ApplyStylesheet2" ; $InputXML ; $XSLTransform )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Canonical
functions.description: Creates canonical xml.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 2 to include comments.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The output mode. Can be 0 for Original C14N 1.0 spec, 1 for Exclusive C14N 1.0 spec or 2 for C14N 1.1 spec.

examples.label: Make canonical:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Canonical"; Canonical XML::Input XML; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ClearVariables
functions.description: Clears the variables. Pass the list returned from XML.SetVariables to clear the variables list.

parameters.name: VariableList
parameters.description: The list of variables.

functions.name: XML.Colorize
functions.description: Formats a XML string with color. This colorizes an existing XML text to show to user, but does not check syntax. Keys get blue, text value black, attributes in purple. Text is returned only with colors. You can apply text font and font size with normal FileMaker functions. Processing time is linear to length, so should be faster than custom functions. Applying text styles takes time. You may consider to skip this if your JSON is e.g. bigger than a million characters to improve performance of your scripts. Colors used for JSON and XML colorization:

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML text to process.

parameters.name: DarkMode
parameters.description: Whether to swap colors to use white for text color. Pass 1 for dark mode and 0 for light mode. Default is 0 for light mode.

examples.label: Colorize a field:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XML.Colorize"; Data::XML) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Data" id="99999" name="XML "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Line numbers, color and format:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.AddLineNumbers"; MBS( "XML.Colorize"; MBS( "XML.Format"; Buckets::Listing)))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Colorize in Auto-Enter calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Colorize"; self)

// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Colorize and format in Auto-Enter calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
xmlFormatted = MBS("XML.Format"; Self);
xmlFormatFailed = MBS("IsError");
xmlText = If(xmlFormatFailed; Self; xmlFormatted);
result = MBS("XML.Colorize"; xmlText)
]; result )

// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets formated and colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Compact
functions.description: Compacts XML document. Removes spaces and returns to make compact representation to save network bandwidth for transmitting or disk space for storage.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier. Add 64 to skip prefix line with xml version.

examples.label: Compact the document:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Compact"; XML::Input XML; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Compact document without prefix:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Compact"; "<a>Test</a>"; 1+64)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.DeletePath
functions.description: Delete a xml node. This function uses a path notation like the JSON functions in FileMaker to describe paths. You can use name of a node followed by option index in square brackets. On the end you can use # followed by a name of an attribute to select only the attribute. Delimiter between items can be dot like FileMaker or newline. If you pass in a XML reference number and you do not pass Flag value 32 for returning as XML, we just modify the XML reference in memory. This avoids performance hit for outputting and parsing xml for each change.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function. If you pass the reference number so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing and xml output it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the node or attribute to query.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 4 to format returned XML. Add 32 to return result as XML.

examples.label: Delete node:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.DeletePath"; "<test><node>Hello</node></test>"; "test.node"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Delete attribute:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "XML.DeletePath"; "<test><node id=\"123\">Hello</node></test>"; "test.node#id"; 0)
Example result:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<test><node>Hello</node></test>
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.DeletePath"; "<test><node id=\"123\">Hello</node></test>"; "test.node#id"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ExtractText
functions.description: Extracts the text from the XML tree. Returns a list with texts from all sub nodes.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

examples.label: Extracts text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "XML.ExtractText"; "<Person><FirstName>Markus</FirstName><LastName>Müller</LastName><City>New York</City></Person>")
Example result:
Markus
Müller
New York
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ExtractText"; "<Person><FirstName>Markus</FirstName><LastName>Müller</LastName><City>New York</City></Person>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Custom function to get xml value as text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([

// GetXMLValue(xml, name)
// returns text of a xml node

part = MBS( "XML.SubTree"; xml; name);
result = If( MBS("IsError"); ""; If(Length(part) = 0; ""; MBS( "XML.ExtractText"; part)))
]; Trim( result))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract text with intermediate XML node:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ExtractText"; "<Person>Hello<br/>World</Person>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Format
functions.description: Formats XML document. Adds indention and new line characters to format XML for showing to human. See also XML.Compact to reverse the formatting.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier. (new in 6.3) Add 64 to skip prefix line with xml version.

examples.label: Format the document:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Format removing namespace:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

examples.label: Check if XML is valid:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "XML.Format"; "<hello></Hello>";0 )
Example result:
[MBS] Failed to parse XML: Opening and ending tag mismatch: hello line 1 and Hello
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.Format"; "<hello></Hello>";0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Format in Auto-Enter calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Format"; self)

// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Colorize and format in Auto-Enter calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
xmlFormatted = MBS("XML.Format"; Self);
xmlFormatFailed = MBS("IsError");
xmlText = If(xmlFormatFailed; Self; xmlFormatted);
result = MBS("XML.Colorize"; xmlText)
]; result )

// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets formated and colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.GetAttribute
functions.description: Queries text of an attribute.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the attribute to query.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

examples.label: Query attribute value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: XML.GetPathValue
functions.description: Queries value in XML. This function uses a path notation like the JSON functions in FileMaker to describe paths. You can use name of a node followed by option index in square brackets. On the end you can use # followed by a name of an attribute to select only the attribute. Delimiter between items can be dot like FileMaker or newline. For more complex queries, you can use XML.Query instead.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the node or attribute to query. With version 11.4 can include backslashes to escape points in the node name.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 2 to return empty text instead of errors. Add 4 to format returned XML. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier. Add 32 to return result as XML, if it’s more than just a text.

#  open word file
Set Variable [
$wordfile
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.OpenContainer
";
WordFile::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$wordFile
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
#  read xml
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.GetXML
";
$WordFile
; "custom") ]
#
# get property count
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:

( "
XML.NodeCount
";
$xml
; "property" ) ]
#
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value: 0 ]

Loop
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "XML.GetPathValue";
$xml
; "Properties.property[" &

& "]#name"; 1+2 ) ]
Set Variable [

$xml
; "Properties.property[" &

Set Variable [

+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>=

]

#

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  read xml</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.GetXML"; $WordFile; "custom") </Calculation>

    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.GetPathValue"; $xml; "Properties.property[" & $i & "]#name"; 1+2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.GetPathValue"; $xml; "Properties.property[" & $i & "].lpwstr"; 1+2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Custom propety" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$name & ": " & $value </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $i >= $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: XML

parameters.name: Name

functions.name: XML.Import
functions.description: Imports XML and creates tables, fields and records. This function can import any XML into FileMaker tables with all fields and attributes. Returns number of records to be added. This function prefers the import and runs it later at idle time. In a script please call XML.Import and then loop and do script pauses. Then use XML.Import.Status to check regularly for updates. When you get back Finished as status, the script can continue with other work. If you have a XML file you need to import regularly into FileMaker and you have no XSLT to transform it for FileMaker, you can use this plugin function. On the first run, it creates for a sample xml file the required tables and fields. Then you can define any layout or script to process values, e.g. copy into your tables. On further runs the plugin may add more fields (if newer xml file has more fields) and import newer records. All tables have three default fields. _RecordUUID is a unique identifier for the record. _ParentRecordUUID provides a link to the record one level higher in the XML. This can be used to find child/parent records. The _CreationTimeStamp is the creation date, so you can distinguish different imports. As a lot of records with a lot of data is imported and you may not need everything, you may want to put the imports in an extra FileMaker file. You decide if you clear tables before import or you want to keep history of all imports. If the XML has errors like a missing < in the xml, you get an error. If you switch on IgnoreError parameter, the plugin reads everything until the error is reached which may give less records than possible. This function works at idle time which is not supported on server. But you can use the function XML.Import.Work to perform the work instead of the idle handler. This enables importing of data, but not table/field creation. Requires FileMaker 12 or newer. See also XML.Import.SetBaseFields and XML.Import.SetExtraField . We use SQL to create records, so you can check FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL after the import for SQL errors. Please wait for an import to finish before calling JSON.Import or XML.Import again since it will cancel the last import and start a new one. Import fails if a table or field name matches a reserved word in FileMaker like RowId. Please rename entries if needed. Since v13.5 you can explicit ask the plugin to not create UUID fields with the flag value 1024. Be aware, that this prevents us from giving you structural data like which child record belongs to which parent. But your import data may already have primary keys for this. Add flag 512 to ignore duplicate errors in FileMaker's field validation. This is useful if you mark some field as unique in FileMaker with always validation and import the same data again. Since v15.1 you can use flag 2048 to ask the plugin to check field types for the insert operation. If the field is number, we convert text to number and we may replace dot with comma if needed. For time, date and timestamp we ask FileMaker to convert which may depend on the current locale in the script. You may need to yourself to change field type from text to time, date or timestamp if you prefer that as field types.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML data to import.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside.

parameters.name: TableNamePrefix
parameters.description: The prefix to put in front of all tables.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Sum of flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. Add 2 to ignore outer XML wrapper nodes. Add 4 to import flat, so no subtables for included nodes. Add 8 to convert CDATA to text nodes. CData should contain ASCII or UTF-8 text! Add 16 to trim values. Add 128 to skip table creation (since v12.3). Add 256 to skip field creation (since v12.3). Add 512 to ignore duplicate errors for FileMaker's validation (since v13.5). Add 1024 to not create UUID fields (since v13.5). Add 2048 to handle field types. (since 15.1)

examples.label: Imports test.xml into FileMaker:

$text
; Value:

("

"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.xml"; "UTF-8")]
Set Variable [

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
