      <Calculation>MBS("Text.ReadTextFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.xml"; "Windows")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import"; $text; ""; "Import2")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import.Total")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$total</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import.Todo")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$todo</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; ($total - $todo) * 100 / $total)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetBottomText"; "Schritt " & ($total - $todo) & " von " & $total)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import.Status")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$s ≠ "Working"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.GetCancel") = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Hide")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import.Cancel")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Hide")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"XML Import"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r & " " & $s</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.Hide")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Import.Cancel
functions.description: Cancels current import.

functions.name: XML.Import.ErrorCount
functions.description: Queries error counter. Should be zero if we had no errors while importing.

functions.name: XML.Import.Errors
functions.description: Queries list of error messages. We remove duplicates, so you may not see all errors.

functions.name: XML.Import.GetExtraFieldDefinitions
functions.description: Queries the extra field definitions.

functions.name: XML.Import.SetBaseFields
functions.description: Sets the names of the base fields per record. If the names are empty, the field is not created.

parameters.name: RecordUUIDFieldName
parameters.description: The field name for the record UUID field.

parameters.name: CreationTimeStampFieldName
parameters.description: The name for the time stamp field.

parameters.name: ParentRecordUUIDFieldName
parameters.description: The name for the parent record UUID field.

examples.label: Disable all fields:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Import.SetExtraField
functions.description: Sets the extra field to add to all tables. Please call before XML.Import . You can call it again when import loop is done and pass in empty name and value to clear it. Only one extra field and only added when table is created.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the extra field.

parameters.name: FieldValue
parameters.description: The value for this field. Can be any data type, but must match field type.

parameters.name: FieldType
parameters.description: The SQL field type. Default is "VARCHAR" for a text field.

examples.label: Set extra field:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.Import.SetExtraField"; "test"; "Hello"; "VARCHAR") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Import.SetExtraFieldDefinitions
functions.description: Sets the extra field definitions. This are SQL field definitions, which we include in creating a table. If you include SQL syntax errors, you may see a lot of errors logged.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of SQL field defintions. Should include field name (if needed in quotes), the type and other options like e.g. UNIQUE flag.

examples.label: Set two extra fields:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("XML.Import.SetExtraFieldDefinitions"; "ImportRun DECIMAL DEFAULT 1¶ImportLabel VARCHAR DEFAULT 'test'") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Import.SetExtraFieldDefinitions"; "ImportRun DECIMAL DEFAULT 1¶ImportLabel VARCHAR DEFAULT 'test'") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Import.Status
functions.description: Queries status of xml import. Returns Working, Finished or Unknown depending of the state. So before first import, it is unknown. Then with first import it switches to working. Now when it's ready, you get back Finished.

functions.name: XML.Import.Todo
functions.description: Queries how many items are to be done for XML import.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to return. By default we return number of records to create. You can pass here "Tables" to get number of tables, "Fields" to get number of fields or "Records" to get number of records.

functions.name: XML.Import.Total
functions.description: Queries total items to be imported.

functions.name: XML.Import.Work
functions.description: Performs import. Normally we do import on idle while a script pause. But on server this does not work. You can call this method in a loop to do the inserts for the import. But it will not create the tables and fields required for import. If you call XML.Import.Work manually, you may not need a script pause, except if fields and tables should be created. Changed in version 11.3 to use per thread data, so multiple scripts on server can do imports independently. Please make sure you call all JSON.Import functions on the same script, so they can access their own local dataset and not interfere with other scripts doing imports. We use SQL to create records, so you can check FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL after the import for SQL errors.

functions.name: XML.InsertRecords
functions.description: Imports records from XML file. You may need to use various XML functions first to get only the relevant parts of a XML or apply a XLST with XML.ApplyStylesheet first. XML needs to have a root element with record elements inside and values inside. If a field is decimal type, we convert value to numeric. Please make sure your number values use dot or comma to match FileMaker's expectation. Returns number of records inserted or error message. In contrast to XML.Import , XML.InsertRecords doesn't create table or fields. And XML.InsertRecords doesn't go into deeper levels.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. If empty, uses name from XML.

examples.label: Insert record:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.InsertRecords"; "<Records>  <Record>    <Firstname>Joe</Firstname>    <LastName>Miller</LastName>    <Age>63</Age>  </Record></Records>"; 0; Get(FileName); "Kontakte" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ItemRefs
functions.description: Queries entries from XML nodes. Returns list of reference numbers for XML objects. Processing large XML gets faster if you first split the top level items into individual items and then process each on your own. Later pass list to XML.Release to release them all. See JSON.GetArrayItems for JSON.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove namespaces.

XML.Release
";
$list

functions.name: XML.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of current XML objects. Returns IDs for all XML objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call XML.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ListAttributes
functions.description: Lists attribute names.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

examples.label: Query attribute names:
examples.text_code: MBS ( "XML.ListAttributes"; "" ) Example result: width height alt
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ListAttributes"; "<img width=\"123\" height=\"456\" alt=\"test\"></img>" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.NodeCount
functions.description: Counts nodes with given name. Only the top nodes in the XML and not sub nodes down the hierarchy. Does not count root node, only first level of children.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the node to query. This can be a list to go over several steps to the target tree part.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

examples.label: Query node count:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeCount"; "<Items><ID>1</ID><ID>3</ID><ID>5</ID></Items>"; "ID")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.NodeNames
functions.description: Queries list of node names in an XML. Excludes the root node. The list has no duplicates. The error "xmlParseEntityRef: no name" means you have an unescaped & in the XML and you should replace it with "&".

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Recursive
parameters.description: Whether to recurse in sub tree. Pass 1 for rescursive or 0 for non-recursive. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 16 to ignore empty tags. (new in plugin version 7.1)

examples.label: Query list of node names:
examples.text_code:
MBS

examples.label: Create records and fill fields with xml values:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [“XML Parser”]
# find the node with all the records we need:
Set Variable [
$oxml
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.SubTree
";
XML Parser::Input
XML; "Body¶GetCategoryProductsResponse" )]
# query number of products
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.NodeCount
";
$oxml
; "PRODUCT")]
# loop over products
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:0]
Loop
# get a product
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.SubTree
";
$oxml
; "PRODUCT";
$index
)]
# get the names of the XML nodes
Set Variable [
$names
; Value:
MBS
( "XML.NodeNames";
$xml
; 0; 16)]
# loop over the names
Set Variable [
$NameCount
; Value:ValueCount (
$names
)]
Set Variable [
$NameIndex
; Value:1]
New Record/Request
Loop
# Query a value and put it in a field
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue(
$names
;
$NameIndex
)]
Set Field By Name ["XML Parser::" &
$name
; GetXMLValue(
$xml
;
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$NameIndex
; Value:
$NameIndex
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$NameIndex
≥
$NameCount
]
End Loop
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="XML Parser"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find the node with all the records we need:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SubTree"; XML Parser::Input XML; "Body¶GetCategoryProductsResponse" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$oxml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query number of products</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeCount"; $oxml; "PRODUCT")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over products</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get a product</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SubTree"; $oxml; "PRODUCT"; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get the names of the XML nodes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeNames"; $xml; 0; 16)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$names</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over the names</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query a value and put it in a field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($names; $NameIndex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="147" name="Set Field By Name">
    <Result>
      <Calculation>$name)</Calculation>
    </Result>
    <TargetName>
      <Calculation>"XML Parser::" & $name; GetXMLValue( $xml</Calculation>
    </TargetName>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$NameIndex + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$NameIndex ≥ $NameCount</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index  ≥ $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Parse
functions.description: Parses a XML document. The document stays in memory and can be queried several times as needed. If you need to query a lot of values, please parse once first. Else all XML functions need to parse again on each call. Please use XML.Release later to free document from memory.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier.

functions.name: XML.Query
functions.description: Performs an XPath query. Result is either simple text, a xml node or several xml nodes in a result root node. XML is returned formatted. Please check on the web for XPath manuals and tutorials. Version 3.4 of the plugins now also return string, number and boolean results instead of just XML nodes. If the query to find the node is too difficult, you can of course use text functions instead. For example use Text.FindBetween and Text.DecodeFromXML . See XML Path Language (XPath) specification here: https://www.w3.org/TR/1999/REC-xpath-19991116/ Only supports version 1.0 or XPath. If you need more, please check the Saxon functions.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML you'd like to parse. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The XPath you'd like to query.

parameters.name: namespaceList
parameters.description: List of namespaces. This is a list of known namespaces in "<prefix1>=<href1> <prefix2>=<href2> ..." format. Version 11..6 and newer will remove quotes surrounding the URLs.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 2 to return a list of xml nodes as text instead of xml. (new in 5.4) Add 4 if you do not like the XML to be formatted. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier. (new in 6.3) Add 16 to return only first item of a list in result. (new in 11.3) Add 32 to return a list of XML entries without a wrapping <result> entry. (new in 11.3)

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

email of allEmails; Value:

functions.name: XML.ReadContainer
functions.description: Reads container content as XML. The document stays in memory and can be queried several times as needed. If you need to query a lot of values, please parse once first. Else all XML functions need to parse again on each call. Please use XML.Release later to free document from memory. By passing XML directly from file content to XML engine, we avoid any corruption due to wrong encoding or line endings. Text encoding is defined in the XML itself in the header. Default is UTF-8.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container value to read as XML file.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier.

examples.label: Read XML from container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ReadContainer"; Test::XMLContainer )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ReadFile
functions.description: Reads a file as XML. The document stays in memory and can be queried several times as needed. If you need to query a lot of values, please parse once first. Else all XML functions need to parse again on each call. Not limited in file size, so you can read in giga byte big files if you have free memory. Please use XML.Release later to free document from memory. By passing XML directly from file content to XML engine, we avoid any corruption due to wrong encoding or line endings. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The file path or URL to read as XML file.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier.

examples.label: Read XML file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ReadFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/iTunes Library.xml")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xmlRef</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Release
functions.description: Releases an XML object.

parameters.name: XMLRef
parameters.description: A XML reference number.

functions.name: XML.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all XML objects. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.SetPathCData
functions.description: Sets path value with CData. If needed, new nodes / attributes are added. This function uses a path notation like the JSON functions in FileMaker to describe paths. Please point with path to new node. You can use name of a node followed by optional index in square brackets. On the end you can use # followed by a name of an attribute to select only the attribute. Empty brackets or too big index allow you to append new node. Delimiter between items can be dot like FileMaker or newline. If you pass in a XML reference number and you do not pass Flag value 32 for returning as XML, we just modify the XML reference in memory. This avoids performance hit for outputting and parsing xml for each change.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the node or attribute to query.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 4 to format returned XML. Add 32 to return result as XML.

parameters.name: CData
parameters.description: The new content of CData.

examples.label: Adds a html encoded name:
examples.text_code: MBS ("XML.SetPathCData"; ""; "export¶person¶name"; 0; "John Miller") Example result: John Miller]]>
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.SetPathCData"; "<export><person></person></export>"; "export¶person¶name"; 0; "<b>John Miller</b>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.SetPathValue
functions.description: Sets a xml node. If needed, new nodes / attributes are added. This function uses a path notation like the JSON functions in FileMaker to describe paths. You can use name of a node followed by option index in square brackets. On the end you can use # followed by a name of an attribute to select only the attribute. Empty brackets or too big index allow you to append new node in v8.1. Delimiter between items can be dot like FileMaker or newline. If you pass in a XML reference number and you do not pass Flag value 32 for returning as XML, we just modify the XML reference in memory. This avoids performance hit for outputting and parsing xml for each change.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the node or attribute to query.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 4 to format returned XML. Add 32 to return result as XML.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value for node or attribute. This is for passing text to be used in XML and encoded properly. If you pass new value as XML, please use XML.SetPathXML instead!.

examples.label: Add a first name:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: XML.SetPathXML
functions.description: Sets path value with XML sub tree. If needed, new nodes / attributes are added. This function uses a path notation like the JSON functions in FileMaker to describe paths. Please point with path to new node. You can use name of a node followed by optional index in square brackets. On the end you can use # followed by a name of an attribute to select only the attribute. Empty brackets or too big index allow you to append new node. Delimiter between items can be dot like FileMaker or newline. If you pass in a XML reference number and you do not pass Flag value 32 for returning as XML, we just modify the XML reference in memory. This avoids performance hit for outputting and parsing xml for each change.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the node or attribute to query.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 4 to format returned XML. Add 32 to return result as XML.

parameters.name: NewXML
parameters.description: The new xml for node or attribute. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

examples.label: Insert new node:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.SetPathXML"; "<export><person></person></export>"; "export¶person¶name"; 0; "<name>Test</name>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.SetSaveNoEmptyTags
functions.description: Sets whether to include close tags for empty nodes. Pass 1 to include the end tags or 0 (default) to shorten them. The setting is used by all XML functions in our plugin.

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: The new flag value.

examples.label: Try enabled:
examples.text_code: MBS ("XML.SetSaveNoEmptyTags"; 1) & MBS ( " XML.Format "; "") Example result: OK
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.SetSaveNoEmptyTags"; 1) & MBS( "XML.Format"; "<c><a></a></c>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try disabled:

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.SetVariables
functions.description: Creates local variables in script for XML nodes. Returns list of variable names. If variables receive multiple values the name in the result comes the highest index in square brackets. You can call XML.ClearVariables later to clear those variables, but at the end of the script all variables will go away. Variable names are shortened in v12.5 and newer to a maximum of 100 characters.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Recursive
parameters.description: Whether to recurse in sub tree. Pass 1 for rescursive or 0 for non-recursive. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to use for the variables names.

examples.label: Set variables:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "XML.SetVariables"; "<Person><FirstName>Markus</FirstName><LastName>Müller</LastName><City>New York</City></Person>")
Example result:
LastName
City
FirstName
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SetVariables"; "<Person><FirstName>Markus</FirstName><LastName>Müller</LastName><City>New York</City></Person>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.SubTree
functions.description: Extracts a given subtree. You can pass an index to get nth item. Returns empty result if index is too high, so you can loop until you get empty result.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the node to query. This can be a list to go over several steps to the target tree part.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 4 if you do not like the XML to be formatted. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier. (new in 6.3)

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the item to get. First has index 0 as this counts down how many to skip.

examples.label: Query person entry:

( "XML.SubTree"; "<People><Person><FirstName>Markus</FirstName><LastName>Müller</LastName><City>New York</City></Person></People>"; "Person")

examples.label: Custom function to get xml value as text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([

// GetXMLValue(xml, name)
// returns text of a xml node

part = MBS( "XML.SubTree"; xml; name);
result = If( MBS("IsError"); ""; If(Length(part) = 0; ""; MBS( "XML.ExtractText"; part)))
]; Trim( result))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create records and fill fields with xml values:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [“XML Parser”]
# find the node with all the records we need:
Set Variable [
$oxml
; Value:
MBS
( "XML.SubTree";
XML Parser::Input
XML; "Body¶GetCategoryProductsResponse" )]
# query number of products
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.NodeCount
";
$oxml
; "PRODUCT")]
# loop over products
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:0]
Loop
# get a product
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "XML.SubTree";
$oxml
; "PRODUCT";
$index
)]
# get the names of the XML nodes
Set Variable [
$names
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.NodeNames
";
$xml
; 0; 16)]
# loop over the names
Set Variable [
$NameCount
; Value:ValueCount (
$names
)]
Set Variable [
$NameIndex
; Value:1]
New Record/Request
Loop
# Query a value and put it in a field
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue(
$names
;
$NameIndex
)]
Set Field By Name ["XML Parser::" &
$name
; GetXMLValue(
$xml
;
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$NameIndex
; Value:
$NameIndex
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$NameIndex
≥
$NameCount
]
End Loop
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="XML Parser"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find the node with all the records we need:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SubTree"; XML Parser::Input XML; "Body¶GetCategoryProductsResponse" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$oxml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query number of products</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeCount"; $oxml; "PRODUCT")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over products</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get a product</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SubTree"; $oxml; "PRODUCT"; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get the names of the XML nodes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeNames"; $xml; 0; 16)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$names</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over the names</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query a value and put it in a field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($names; $NameIndex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="147" name="Set Field By Name">
    <Result>
      <Calculation>$name)</Calculation>
    </Result>
    <TargetName>
      <Calculation>"XML Parser::" & $name; GetXMLValue( $xml</Calculation>
    </TargetName>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$NameIndex + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NameIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$NameIndex ≥ $NameCount</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index > $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.Tidy
functions.description: Performs tidy on XML. Sets error log, which can be queried with Text.TidyErrorLog function. Returns new XML back after repairing. For options, check this website: https://api.html-tidy.org/tidy/tidylib_api_5.8.0/tidy_quickref.html Use XML.Colorize to apply colors if needed. By default we apply options to use utf-8 for input and output as encoding. Line endings use Char(13) as FileMaker does and we disable the tidy mark for the html header.

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: The input XML.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The tidy options as JSON object.

examples.label: Cleanup some XML:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.Tidy"; "<title>Test")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ToHTML
functions.description: Converts XML to HTML. We build for you a HTML with tables for each array and object. We include values and tag rows with even/odd CSS classes. See also XML.ToJSON function to convert to JSON.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML you'd like to convert. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times queries to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for processing. Currently you can pass 1 to exclude html header and just get a HTML table.

parameters.name: CSS Style
parameters.description: Extra CSS / HTML to include.

examples.label: Convert XML to HTML:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  html = MBS("XML.ToHTML"; XML to HTML::XML Input; 0; XML to HTML::CSS); 
  // now use XML colorization to color HTML:
  html = MBS("XML.Colorize"; html; XML to HTML::Dark Mode) 
]; html)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ToJSON
functions.description: Converts XML to JSON. For nodes in the XML we create objects and key/values. Attributes are put in @attributes nodes. Namespaces are stripped. Added option flag 2 in version 10.5 to not use @attributes object, but put attributes directly in objects with "@" prefix. The JSON.ToXML function unpacks that. Added option flags 4 and 8 for version 11.2 to handle boolean and numbers. Added option flag 16 in version 12.1 to include namespaces.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 2 to put attributs inline. Add 4 to make entries with true/false being boolean. Add 8 to make entries with numbers as numbers. Add 16 to include namespaces. Add 32 to use empty text for empty xml nodes.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$XML
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.ToXML
"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}"; "test") ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$xml
]
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
( "XML.ToJSON";
$XML
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$json
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.ToXML"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}"; "test") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

functions.name: XML.Validate
functions.description: Validates XML against schema. Please pass in XML (or reference number from XML.Parse ) and a valid XSD schema. Version 8.2 or newer will return list of errors, so you see them all. This function may look into the current directory for referenced xml/xsd files. See Process.SetCurrentDirectory function. If you need more validation, check out Saxon.Validate function.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML to process. Can be XML as text or the reference returned by XML.Parse function, so you can make several times changes to the XML without parsing it each time.

parameters.name: Schema
parameters.description: The schema definition.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various Flags. Add 1 to ignore errors in xml and continue parsing. This may lead to not everything in the xml being read. Add 8 to remove all namespaces before query to have queries easier.

examples.label: Validate XML:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("XML.Validate";
XML Validation::XML
;
XML Validation::Schema
; 0) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.Validate"; XML Validation::XML; XML Validation::Schema; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XML.ValidateFile
functions.description: Validates XML against schema. Please pass in XML file path and a valid XSD schema. Return list of errors, so you see them all. If you use Process.SetCurrentDirectory to change current folder for the application process, the library uses this folder to search for referenced xsd files. Changed with version 10.2 that you can pass schema as file name/path, so we can load it from same folder as the schema and referenced files in same folder are found. If you need more validation, check out Saxon.Validate function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: Native file path to the file to check.

parameters.name: Schema
parameters.description: The schema definition as xml text. With version 10.2 can also be file path.

examples.label: Validate a file in a folder:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Process.SetCurrentDirectory
"; "/Users/cs/Documents/todo/XML Validate/") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("XML.ValidateFile"; "test.xml";
XML Validation::Schema
) ]
Set Field [
XML Validation::Result
;
$result
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Process.SetCurrentDirectory"; "/Users/cs/Documents/todo/XML Validate/") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.ValidateFile"; "test.xml"; XML Validation::Schema) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    <Field table="XML Validation" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Validate all in one folder:

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Process.SetCurrentDirectory
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/XML Validate") ]
#

;
MBS

# folder contains xmldsig-core-schema.xsd, xenc-schema.xsd, generalInvoiceRequest_450.xsd and test.xml

  </Step>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> validate file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XML.ValidateFile"; "test.xml"; "generalInvoiceRequest_450.xsd") </Calculation>
    <Field table="XML Validation" id="99999" name="Result "/>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

functions.name: ZipFile.CRCFile
functions.description: Calculates CRC of a file. This is same CRC as used in zip files. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path.

examples.label: Calculate CRC of a file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.CRCFile"; "C:\test.txt" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.Close
functions.description: Closes the zip archive.

parameters.name: Comment
parameters.description: The comment to include in the form

examples.label: Close the archive with comment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.Close"; "Archive created using MBS Plugin" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.SetCompressionLevel
functions.description: Sets the compression level. By default the plugin uses maximum compression level.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description: The compression method to use. Can be 0 for no compression and 1 for deflate compression.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: The compression level from 0 (fastest) to 9 (maximum).

examples.label: Set default settings:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.SetCompressionLevel"; 1; 9 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.UsePassword
functions.description: Sets the password to use for compressing. The password is needed to create encrypted files in archive. Each item in archive could have its own password, but normally all have the same.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password text.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for Password parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Set password:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.UsePassword"; "Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.WriteContainer
functions.description: Writes a file to the zip archive with content from a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to pick the data.

examples.label: Creates a text file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.CreateFile"; "test.jpg"; $date )
MBS( "ZipFile.WriteContainer"; $container )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.WriteFile
functions.description: Reads a file and writes it to the zip archive. Please use ZipFile.CreateFile first to create a new file before writing to it. This works with all types of files. See FileDialog functions if you like to show a save dialog to pick a destination for the file path. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to read and put into the archive.

examples.label: Creates a file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.CreateFile"; "test.txt"; $date )
MBS( "ZipFile.WriteFile"; "/tmp/test.txt" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.WriteHex
functions.description: Writes data into the zip archive. The data is encoded as hex string, so you can pass any byte values as a text. The hex string is decoded before writing. Please use ZipFile.CreateFile first to create a new file before writing to it.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to write.

examples.label: Creates a text file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.CreateFile"; "test.txt"; $date )
MBS( "ZipFile.WriteHex"; "48656C6C6F20576F726C64" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ZipFile.WriteText
functions.description: Writes a text into the zip archive. This is a way to easily add a text file to a zip archive. Please use ZipFile.CreateFile first to create a new file before writing to it. In version 5.0 of the plugin, the parameters are changed and we got an extra unused parameter between function name and text. This was corrected in 5.1pr3.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to write.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Creates a text file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ZipFile.CreateFile"; "test.txt"; $date )
MBS( "ZipFile.WriteText"; "Hello World" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
